ref: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”

text: ka Josiya mɩ ɩ Jekoniya sãa, nɩ a wʊ yɛɛr a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban bar a ba tẽe tɩ chen tɩ kpɩɛrɛ Babilon tẽe.

ref: From the exile to the birth of Jesus, his ancestors were: Jehoiachin, Shealtiel, Zerubbabel, Abiud, Eliakim, Azor, Zadok, Achim; Eliud, Eleazar, Matthan, Jacob, and Joseph, the husband of Mary, the mother of Jesus, who is called the Messiah.
text: Jekɔb mɩ lɛ ɩ a Josefʊ sãa na ɩ Mɛɛr sɩrɛ 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Yesu ma, a Yesu nyã lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

ref: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
text: ) a par lɛ, Nãaŋmɩn ben a sɩ sɛ̃.

ref: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
text: Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto dɔɔfʊ yele tu. A wʊ ma Mɛɛr ŋmaan na Josefʊ baraa tɩchɛ baa kul taa sɛrɛ ɛ, ka Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ vɛ̃ ka wʊ tara pʊɔ.

ref: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
text: A wʊ baraa Josefʊ na ɩ nɩtor jũu, ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ su wʊ vĩ nɩyɔɔ pɔ ɛ, ka wʊ tɩɛr ɩka wʊ sɔɔl bar wʊ jaa jomm.

ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Wʊn wa tɩɛr a yele nyã maalfʊ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn malaka wa a wʊ sɛ̃ jãn pɔ wa 'yɛr, “Josefʊ, David bie, ta joro dãbãɛ ɩka fʊ de a Mɛɛr ka wʊ ɩ a fʊ pɔɔ ɛ, a bie na be a wʊ pʊɔm yin a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kpɛ̃ɔ pɔ.

ref: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
text: Wʊn dɔɔn dabile ka fʊ na bʊɔlɔ a wʊ yuor Yesu, 'lɔ lɛ na faa a wʊ nɩbɛ yin a ba yeldɛbɛ pɔ.”

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Anyã a jaa ɩ naa ka a wiil a sɩ Soro 'yɛrbie wʊn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr tu a wʊ 'yɛ'yɛrɛ pɔ ɩ yelmãɛ,

ref: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
text: Josefʊ na wa jɛɔ a gʊ̃ɔ pɔ, wʊ ɩn a lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn malaka na 'yɛr ɩka wʊ ɩ. Ka wʊ de a Mɛɛr ka wʊ ɩ a wʊ pɔɔ,

ref: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
text: tɩchɛ baa lɔɔ sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn dɔɔ a dabile, na wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ yuor Yesu.

ref: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
text: A Nãa Hɛrɔd na wa wõ a lɛ wʊ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ naa, ka Jerusalɛm nɩyɔɔ pʊʊr mɩ sɔ̃ɔ,

ref: Herod brought together the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses and asked them, “Where will the Messiah be born?”
text: be lɛ wʊ bʊɔl a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɛɛ wiwiilbe sʊʊr bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ bɔ̃ɔ a jie 'lɔ ban na dɔɔ a Nɩ-iraa.

ref: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
text: ‘Fʊ̃ʊ Bɛtɩlɛhɛm na be a Judiya paal pɔ, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ jaa pʊʊrbile a Juda kpãɛn dẽme pɔ, fʊ pɔ lɛ a kpɛ̃ɔ sʊɔ na yi kaara a n nɩbɛ Isɩral.’ ” (Mika

ref: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
text: Be lɛ ka Hɛrɔd sɔɔl bʊɔl a yɛ̃ dẽme bala sʊʊr ba a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a ŋmɛrbir na pur wa.

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: Be lɛ wʊ tara ba chiin Bɛtɩlɛhɛm tɩ 'yɛr, “Yɩ chen tɩ yɔbɔ a bie kaa nɛ nɛ, yin wa nyɛ wʊ yɩ lɩɛb wa 'yɛr kʊ ma ka n mɩ chen tɩ puor wʊ.”

ref: The wise men listened to what the king said and then left. And the star they had seen in the east went on ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
text: Ban wa chɛl a nãa 'yɛr baar, ba yin lo a sɔr chiine, a ŋmɛrbir 'lɔ ban nyɛ a sapar de ban nie tɩ ara a jie 'lɔ a bie na be.

ref: They were thrilled and excited to see the star.
text: Ban wa nyɛ ka a ŋmɛrbir ara nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ban yɔɔ jaa.

ref: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
text: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn dɔ̃ɔ kpãa ba a jãn pɔ, ɩka ba ta lɛ lɩɛb chen a Nãa Hɛrɔd sɛ̃ ɛ, ka ba tu jiyuo kul a ba tẽe.

ref: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
text: A yɛ̃ bɔ̃ɔnbɔ na wa chen baar, a Nãaŋmɩn malaka wan Josefʊ sɛ̃ jãn pɔ wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma, na fʊ jo chiin be Ijipitɩ wɛr pɔ, na fʊ tɩ be a be wa tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ɩn na 'yɛr kʊ fʊ, bojũu a Hɛrɔd nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ bʊɔrɔ na a bie kʊfʊ.”

ref: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
text: Ka a Josefʊ ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma a tãsɔɔ bãɩn jo chiin be a Ijipitɩ.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: A Nãa Hɛrɔd na wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a yɛ̃ dẽme banyã na yɩ a sapar bɛl wʊ naa a suur kpɛ wʊ a wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ sojasɩ ɩka ba chen tɩ kʊ dabile jaa na be a Bɛtɩlɛhɛm na ta yuom ayi bɩɩ na ba ta sɛrɛ ɛ, yi a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ jaa wʊn sʊʊr bɔ̃ɔn yi a yɛ̃ bɔ̃ɔnbɔ sɛ̃.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: “Ba wõn kɔkɔr be a Wurama tẽe pɔ, kõyiel nɩ wuur kpãɛn, Wurachɛl lɛ kõnõ a wʊ bibiir 'yɔ̃ɔ. Ba kpi na, waa lɛ bʊɔrɔ 'maalfʊ ɛ bojũu baa lɛ chɛnɛ go ɛ.” (Jeremiya

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Hɛrɔd na wa kpi, Nãaŋmɩn malaka wan Josefʊ sɛ̃ be a Ijipitɩ jãn pɔ wa 'yɛr,

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: “Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma na fʊ chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ, a bala na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ a bie kʊfʊ kpi naa.”

ref: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
text: Josefʊ vaan ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ.

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: Tɩchɛ wʊn wa wõ ɩka Archelaus lɛ di a nãa a Judiya a wʊ sãa kũu puor, wʊ joro na dãbãɛ ɩka wʊ lɛ chen a be. Ka a Nãaŋmɩn wa kpãa wʊ a jãn pɔ ɩka wʊ chen be Galilee paal pɔ.

ref: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
text: Be lɛ wʊ chen Najaretɩ tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a be, ka a lɛ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩ yelmãɛ, “Ban bʊɔl wʊ naa Najaretɩ nɩrɛ.”

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: Yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ lɛ befʊ wiil ɩka yɩ lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr.

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: 'yɛrɛ, “Yɩ lɩɛb a yɩ befʊ, a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ ta na.”

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Nyɔɔma kɔɔlʊ fuu lɛ Jɔɔn ma su tɩ de gãn a lũn a sɩɛ. Soor nɩ sɩkãa lɛ ɩ a wʊ bʊ̃diir.

ref: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
text: Nɩyɔɔ yin a Jerusalɛm nɩ Judiya chiine a wʊ sɛ̃ nɩ a bala na yi a Jordan man koor.

ref: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
text: Ba woro naa a ba yeldɛbɛ tɩ wʊ suoro ba a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ be a Jordan man pɔ.

ref: Many Pharisees and Sadducees also came to be baptized. But John said to them: You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
text: Jɔɔn na wa nyɛ a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Saduseemɩne na waara a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ sofʊ jie, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩn waa bibiir, an n 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɩka yɩ jo yi a Nãaŋmɩn suur na waara mɔ̃?

ref: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
text: A 'lar dɔɔl a tɩɛ pĩlem gu a ŋmaafʊ, tɩɛ jaa ba wa wɔ̃nɔ̃ wɔ̃ sʊ̃n ɛ ban ŋmaa wʊn lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ bũu pɔ.

ref: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
text: A ŋmayɩɛlaa ben a wʊ nũu pɔ, ka wʊ na yɩɛl a mʊ̃ɩ a vafʊ jie na wʊ kpaar a bie 'yɔ̃ɔ a boor pɔ tɩ chʊm a sɔ̃sɔɔr nɩ bũu 'lɔ na ba tara kpĩinfʊ bar.”

ref: Jesus left Galilee and went to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
text: Yesu yin a be a Galilee chen a Jordan man pɔ, ka a Jɔɔn na tɩ so wʊ a kʊ̃ɔ.

ref: But John kept objecting and said, “I ought to be baptized by you. Why have you come to me?”
text: Jɔɔn ba ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ so wʊ ɛ, na wʊ 'yɛr fʊ̃ʊ lɛ sɛɛ ɩka fʊ so ma tɩchɛ ka fʊ lɛ wa a n sɛ̃ go?

ref: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
text: Tɩchɛ ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Vɛ̃ ka a ɩ a lɛ a pʊ̃pãanyã, a vɩɛl na ɩka sɩ ɩ a lɛ jaa Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ.” Ka Jɔɔn sɔɔ lɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr.

ref: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
text: A lɛ wʊn wa so a Yesu a kʊ̃ɔ baar, a wʊ yi a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ, a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ wʊ nyɛ a saaju na yuo a wʊ nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na kaara ŋmam kaar suu wa mar a Yesu ju.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Tɩ a kɔkɔr yɩ a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ a saaju 'yɛrɛ, “A anyãna lɛ ɩ a n binʊ̃nãa ka a n 'yɔ̃ɔ ma 'maa nɩ wʊ.”

ref: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
text: Yesu na wa wõ ɩka ba nyɔɔn Jɔɔn na ba pɔɔ, wʊ chen Galilee.

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: Wʊ yin Najaretɩ na wʊ tɩ kpɩɛrɛ be a Kapenahum a man nɛɛ be Jebulun nɩ Naftalɩ wɛr pɔ.

ref: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
text: Ka a Yesu mɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “A mɩ sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, ta bɛɛr a yɩ Nãaŋmɩn kaa ɛ.” (Mosesɩ Gãma Na Tu Anũu

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
text: A tɩɩ lɛ tara wʊn don tɔ̃ɔ ju tɩ wiil wʊ a nãa di jiir a jaa na be ka a wɛr pɔ, na a bʊ̃vɩɛlsɩ lɛ an be.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,
text: Ka a lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Isaiya na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩ yelmãɛ,

ref: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
text: “Jebulun wɛr, Naftalɩ wɛr, a man sɔr a Jordan man gɔ̃ɔn. Galilee be a nɩyor na kpɩɛrɛ.

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Ka ba tɔ̃ɔ tãɛ a lɔyaarsɩ bar tɩ na ba turo wʊ.

ref: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
text: Yesu na wa de a Galilee man gogoro lana, wʊ nyɛn yɛɛr ayi Simon ban bʊɔlɔ Pita nɩ a wʊ yɛbɛ Andiru na lɔɔrɔ lɔyaar 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ. Ba dɔ̃ɔ ɩ jãnyɔɔrbɔ.

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
text: Wʊn lɛ yɩ a be chiine, wʊ lɛ nyɛn yɛɛr ayi, Jemesɩ na ɩ Jebedee bidaba naa a wʊ yɛbɛ Jɔɔn ka ba be a gboro pɔ nɩ a ba sãa Jebedee, maala a ba lɔyaarsɩ ka wʊ bʊɔl ba.

ref: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
text: A ba tɔ̃ɔ tɩɛ a gboro bar nɩ a ba sãa tɩ turo wʊ.

ref: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Yesu yɔn a Galilee wile ba a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir tɩ mʊɔlɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ tɩ sãana nɩrɛ jaa baal.

ref: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
text: Ka a wʊ yele yaar a Siriya ka ba tara a baalsɩ ba jaa waan a wʊ sɛ̃, bala a baalsɩ nɩ ãgãn ɔbɔ na nyɔɔ, bala na tara a sɩdɛbɛ, banyã baal tãanaa na nyɔɔ nɩ gbɛrfʊ jaa bʊrɔ, ka wʊ sãa a ba baalsɩ.

ref: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
text: Nɩyɔɔ yin Galilee, nɩ Dekapolisɩ, nɩ Jerusalɛm, nɩ Judiya nɩ a Jordan man gɔ̃ɔn wa turo wʊ.

ref: So if you are about to place your gift on the altar and remember that someone is angry with you,
text: “Ala wa ɩ fʊ waara nɩ naa a fʊ kʊfʊ, a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie na fʊ wa tɩɛr tɔ ɩka fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a yɛbɛ tara na 'yɛr,

ref: and he taught them:
text: A wʊ piel wile ba 'yɛrɛ,

ref: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
text: “Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ a yɩn banyãna na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, tɩ bʊɔrɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ. Yɩn lɛ so a saaju nãalʊ̃ʊ.

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
text: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ a yɩn na kõnõ, Nãaŋmɩn na 'maan a yɩ nyãa.

ref: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
text: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ a yɩn na siir a yɩ mãɛ. Nãaŋmɩn na kʊ ban bon jaa na be a wɛr pɔ.

ref: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
text: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ yɩn banyã na kaara nɩrɛ nɩbaal, Nãaŋmɩn mɩ na kãan a yɩ nɩbaal.

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ banyã na nʊ̃ Nãaŋmɩn sɩrɛ, nɩ a ba nyãa wʊ jaa, bala lɛ na nyɛ Nãaŋmɩn.

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
text: Tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩ ta pɔ jaa ɛ, yɩ ta pɔn a saaju gba ɛ, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn jaa jie lɛ,

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
text: bɩɩ a wɛr ju, a wʊ gbɛr dɔɔlfʊ jie lɛ bɩɩ Jerusalɛm ɛ, bojũu be lɛ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ kpɩɛrɛ.

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
text: Nɩrɛ maa chʊm fãtɩn de pɩɛ pɔɔ ɛ tɩchɛ wʊ ma bin a kuorgbe ju kʊ chaar yɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ jaa a yir pɔ.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: “Yɩ ta tɩɛr ɩka n wan ɩka n wa sɔ̃ɔ a nɛbinãa bɩɩ, a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ wiilfʊ bar ɛ, n ba wa ɩka n wa sɔ̃ɔ yaa bar ɛ, n wan ɩka n vɛ̃ ka a ɩ yelmãɛ.

ref: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
text: Tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, ala ɩ fʊ nyɛn na yɛbɛ suur, wʊn nyɛn 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ, ala ɩ fʊ tʊ̃n yɛbɛ na nyɛn 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ a nɩbɛrɛ niem, fʊ̃ʊ wa 'yɛr nɩrɛ ɩka, jɔl nyã, na kpɛ̃n a bũu dagel 'lɔ pɔ.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: “An ɩ yɩn nɩ a yɩ sãn sʊɔ lɛ chiine a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie, yɩ mʊlɔ wʊ a sɔr pɔ, ka lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, wʊn de yɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ a 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ nũu pɔ, ka wʊ de yɩ kʊ a nɩpɔɔraa ka wʊ mɩ pɔɔ yɩ bar.

ref: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
text: Ala be a lɛ, faa be na wa yi ka fʊ̃ʊ ba wa yab a sãn baar ɛ.

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: “A mɩ dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩka, nɩrɛ nyã jaa na tɔr a wʊ pɔɔ bar, wʊ kʊ wʊ a pɔɔ bar gãn. (Mosesɩ Gãma Na Tu Anũu

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Yɩ mɩ lɛ wõn ban dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne, ‘Yɩ taa sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a nɛɛ 'lɔ yɩn põl 'yɔ̃ɔ a yɩ mãɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ tara a lɛ yɩn põl 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ Soro.’

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ ɔ̃ɔ ɩ ɔ̃ɔ ka a yɩ kai ɩ kai, bon 'lɔ na gar a nɩtɔ̃ yin a dɛɛr sʊɔ sɛ̃.

ref: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
text: “Yɩ dɔ̃ɔ wõ ban 'yɛr ɩka nɩbir na yãan nɩbir sãn, ka nyɩm mɩ yaa nyɩm sãn. (A Yifʊ Gãn

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Tɩchɛ maa 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, ta pii a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sɔ̃ɔ fʊ sɔr ɛ. Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ wa 'ma fʊ a tʊkpal nyã pɔ, lɩɛb a nyã lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ.

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ wa fɩr fʊ ɩka fʊ de a wʊ tuor tuo chiin niem bãlãa, tuo chiin tɛɛr jaa.

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Kʊ nɩrɛ jaa na sʊɔr fʊ, ta mɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ nyã mɩ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ pɛ̃ɛ a fʊ bon ɛ.

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: “Yɩ wõn ban dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩka, yɩ nʊ̃ a yɩ tɔ sʊɔ tɩ 'laar a yɩ dɔ̃sʊɔ. (Mosesɩ Gãma Na Tu Ata

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: Tɩchɛ maa 'lɔ 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, nʊ̃ a yɩ dɔ̃dẽme tɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn kʊrɔ a bala na gere yɩ,

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: ka yɩ na ɩ Nãaŋmɩn bibiir bojũu 'lɔ lɛ ma vɛ̃ ka a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ pur 'yɔ̃ɔ a nɩdɛbɛ nɩ a nɩsʊ̃n tɩ na wʊ ma vɛ̃ ka saa wa 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na tor nɩ a bala na ba tor ɛ.

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Yɩn nʊ̃ a bala na nʊ̃ yɩ tɛɛ, bo sãyar lɛ yɩn nyɛ? A lõpodierbe maa mɩ ɩ a lɛ bɩɩ?

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Yɩn ma puoro a bala tɛɛ na ɩ a yɩ nɩbɛ, bo tɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ be be? A bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, maa mɩ ɩ a lɛ bɩɩ?

ref: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
text: Yɩ ɩ nɩmɩn a lɛ a yɩ sãa Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju kaar.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Yɩ bɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ sʊ̃, na yɩ taa mʊɔlɔ a yɩ yeltorsɩ a nɩbɛ niem ɛ, ka ba na bɔ̃ɔn ɛ, yɩn wa ɩrɛ a lɛ, yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ sãyar a yɩ sãa Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju sɛ̃ ɛ.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Yɩn maa wa sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn, yɩ ta ma kaara a nɛɛr ayi dẽme kaar ɛ, ba ma nʊ̃n ɩka ba puoro a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yie pɔ, nɩ a tẽbɛrɛ pɔ, nɩ a sɔchara pɔ, ka nɩbɛ na nyɛ ba. Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, ɩka ba nyɛn a ba sãyar baar.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Tɩchɛ fʊ̃ʊ maa wa sʊɔrɔ a Sãa Nãaŋmɩn nyã sɩn ba nyɛrɛ ɛ, kpɛ a fʊ die pɔ, tɩ pɔɔ a pãn, tɩ sʊɔr a Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na nyɛrɛ bon jaa, na yab fʊn a sãyar.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: Yɩn maa wa sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn, yɩ ta ma 'yɛrɛ 'yɛrbie ala na ba tara tɔ̃nɔ̃ ɛ, lɛ lɛ a bala na ba bɔ̃ɔ Nãaŋmɩn ma maala, ba tɩɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn na wõn a ba sʊɔrfʊ a ba 'yɛrbiyɔɔ jũu.

ref: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
text: Yɩ ta kaara a ba kaar ɛ, a yɩ Sãa den nie bɔ̃ɔn bon 'lɔ yɩn bʊɔrɔ.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: “Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ yɩn ma sʊɔr a Nãaŋmɩn. Sɩ Sãa na be a saaju. Sɩ kʊ naa a fʊ yuor tɩɩr.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: A fʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ wʊ wa. A fʊ bɔfʊ wʊ ɩ a wɛr pɔ, nɩtãa lɛ a fʊ bɔfʊ na ɩrɛ be a saaju.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: “Yɩn wa vɛ̃ a yɩ taaba yeldɛbɛ bar ba, a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju mɩ na vɛ̃n bar yɩ.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Yɩn ba wa vɛ̃ a yɩ taaba yeldɛbɛ kʊ ba ɛ, a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn mɩ kʊ̃ vɛ̃ a yɩ yeldɛbɛ kʊ yɩ ɛ.

ref: Instead, comb your hair and wash your face.
text: Yɩn wa gal kɔ̃, yɩ lor a yɩ nie maal a yɩ mãɛ,

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: ka nɩbɛ ta bɔ̃ɔn yɩn gal kɔ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn nyã sɩn ba nyɛrɛ ɛ, a Sãa Nãaŋmɩn nyã na nyɛrɛ yelsɔɔlsɩ na yab yɩn a sãyar.

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Yɩ de nie bɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nɩ a wʊ yeltorsɩ, tɩchɛ a anyãna a jaa, wʊn den pʊɔ yɩ.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: A lɛ jũu, yɩ ta waala a bio 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, bio lɛ na waala a wʊ mãɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ. Bɩbir jaa tara na a wʊ yelwõnsɩ.

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: “Yɩ ta bine a yɩ nãbonsɩ ka a wɛr ju be kpolo, nɩ sɔrɔ, nɩ na sɔ̃ɔ bar ɛ, nɩ be nãnyisɩ nɩ naa kpɛ ju ɛ.

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Tɩchɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ nãbonsɩ bine a saaju, be lɛ a kpolo nɩ sɔrɔ na kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ sɔ̃ɔ bar ɛ, nɩ be a nãnyisɩ na kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ju ɛ.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: A jie 'lɔ a fʊ nãbonsɩ na be, be lɛ a fʊ tɩɛr mɩ ma be.

ref: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
text: Tɩchɛ a fʊ nɩbir ba wa ɩ ɛ, a fʊ ãgãn wʊ jaa ma ɩn libe. Ala ɩ a chãa 'lɔ na be a fʊ pɔ ɩn libe, nyɛ lɛ a libe na jã.

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: “Nɩrɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ nɩbɛrɛ ayi tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɛ. Wʊn nʊ̃n ãsʊɔ, tɩ 'laar ãsʊɔ, bɩɩ wõnõ a ãsʊɔ toor, tɩ ba wõnõ ãsʊɔ toor ɛ. Yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ kʊ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ libie ɛ.

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: A lɛ jũu, yɩ ta waala 'yɛrɛ ɩka, bo lɛ yɩn di bɩɩ nyũ ɛ, bɩɩ bo lɛ yɩn su ɛ?

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Yɩ kaa a lubil na be a saaju. Ba maa bʊr ɛ, bɩɩ ŋma ɛ, bɩɩ tara boor ma bin ɛ, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ guolo ba. Yaa fɛr gar a lubil bɩɩ?

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Ka bo lɛ vɛ̃ ka yɩ tɩɛrɛ a bʊ̃sursɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ? Yɩ kaa nyɛ a mɔpuul lɛ an ma nʊ̃ɔ, ba maa tʊ̃ ɛ, bɩɩ wob fuu su ɛ,

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: a Nãa Solomon gba nɩ a wʊ tɩɩr wʊ jaa ba dɔ̃ɔ su fuu ka wʊ vɩɛlɛ gar a mɔpuul a nyãna ɛ.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn maal a mɔpuul ka a vɩɛlɛ, tɩchɛ a maa kor ɛ. Nɩbɛ ma chʊm ka a di. A Nãaŋmɩn na ma maal a mɔpuul a nɩtɔ̃, wʊ kaara a yɩn na ɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ gar a mɔpuul anyãna. A yɩ sɔɔfʊ lɛ ba jã bon ɛ.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: A bala na ba puor ɛ, lɛ de a ba tɩɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a anyãna pɔ, a yɩ Sãa bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka yɩ bʊɔrɔ naa a bonsɩ anyãna a jaa.

ref: Don't condemn others, and God won't condemn you.
text: “Yɩ ta tɔɔr nɩrɛ jaa 'yɛr ɛ, ka Nãaŋmɩn mɩ wa tɔɔr a yɩ 'yɛr ɛ.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: Nãaŋmɩn na tɔɔr a yɩ 'yɛr nɩtãa lɛ yɩn tɔɔr a yɩ tɔ sʊɔ 'yɛr. Lɛ fʊn man tɩr, lɛ mɩ lɛ ban man kʊ fʊ.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: “Ŋmɩnɛ a fʊ nyɛ dabile na be a fʊ yɛbɛ nɩbir pɔ tɩ ba chɛlɛ a dakpɛ̃ɛ na be a fʊ̃ʊ nɩbir pɔ ɛ?

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Bɩɩ ŋmɩnɛ fʊn ɩ 'yɛr kʊ a fʊ yɛbɛ vɛ̃ ka ir a dabile na be a fʊ nɩbir pɔ bar tɩ ba chɛlɛ a dakpɛ̃ɛ na be a fʊ nɩbir pɔ ɛ?

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Yɩn nɛɛr ayi dẽme, de nie ir a dakpɛ̃ɛ a fʊ nɩbir pɔ bar na fʊ pãa nyɛrɛ tʊ̃ɔ ir a dabile a fʊ yɛbɛ nɩbir pɔ mɩ bar.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Ala ɩ a yɩn na ɩ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ bɔ̃ɔn bʊ̃vɩla ma maal kʊ a yɩ bibiir, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ an ɩ ka a yɩ Sãa na be a saaju, kʊ̃ de bʊ̃vɩɛl kʊ a bala na sʊɔr wʊ ɛ?

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: “A yɩn na ɩ a sãamɩne, buor sʊɔ bie lɛ na sʊɔr a wʊ sãa bʊ̃diir, ka wʊ de kʊsɩr kʊ wʊ?

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text: “Yɩ sʊɔr, Nãaŋmɩn na kʊ yɩ naa. Yɩ yɔbɔ, yɩn nyɛ naa, yɩ kpoor a pãn, wʊn yuo naa.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔr na nyɛ naa, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na yɔbɔ nyɛ, ka a pãn mɩ yuo kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kpoor.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Bɩɩ ka a bie sʊɔr jãm, ka wʊ de waa kʊ wʊ?

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: A lɛ yɩn ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka a nɩbɛ ba ma maala kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩ mɩ ma maala a lɛ ban bʊɔrɔ kʊrɔ ba, a nyã lɛ ɩ a Nɛbinãa nɩ a 'Yɛ'yɛrbɛ Wiilfʊ.

ref: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
text: “A lɛ jũu, nɩrɛ jaa na wõ a n 'yɛrbie anyãna tɩ maala a lɛ na kaara fɩka yɛ̃ sʊɔ na mɛ a wʊ yir dɔɔl pie ju.

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: A dɔ̃dɔr ɩn jaa fɩɩl ka nyɛvʊr sɔr mɩ ɩ jaa kãrakãra ka nɩbɛ bãlãa na tʊ̃ɔ tu.

ref: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
text: “Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a jire 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ, ba ma bal ba mãɛ piir kaar tɩchɛ ba ɩn mɔdʊ̃ be be na sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: A ba wɔ̃mɔ̃ lɛ yɩn bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban be. Ba ma pɔr 'wɔ̃rɔ gʊtɩɛ ju bɩɩ pɔr kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ nyaartɩɛ ju ɔ?

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Lɛ mɩ lɛ tɩsʊ̃ jaa ma wɔ̃n wɔ̃sʊ̃n ka tɩdɛɛr mɩ ma wɔ̃ wɔ̃dɛbɛ.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Tɩsʊ̃ ba wɔ̃nɔ̃ wɔ̃dɛbɛ ɛ bɩɩ tɩdɛɛr mɩ ba wɔ̃nɔ̃ wɔ̃sʊ̃n ɛ.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Tɩɛ jaa baa wa wɔ̃nɔ̃ wɔ̃sʊ̃n ɛ, ban ŋmaa wʊn lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ bũu pɔ.

ref: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
text: A ba wɔ̃mɔ̃ lɛ yɩn de bɔ̃ɔn ba.

ref: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
text: Tɩchɛ, nɩrɛ jaa na wõ a n 'yɛrbie anyãna tɩ na waa maal ɛ, kaara fɩka jɔl na mɛ a wʊ yir dɔɔl bire ju.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
text: Saa wa naa, ka a kʊ̃ɔ yɩ yaar jilʊ̃ a yir, ka a sɛsɛbɛ fu ŋme a yir kʊ lo ka a wʊ lofʊ ɩ dɛɛr.”

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: “A ba ɩ nɩrɛ jaa na bʊɔlɔ ɩka n Soro, n Soro, lɛ na kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ nyã na maala a lɛ a n Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju na bʊɔrɔ.

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
text: A bɩbir 'lɔ daar, nɩyɔɔ na 'yɛr kʊ ma n Soro, n Soro, saa 'yɛr Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr a fʊ yuor pɔ ɛ, na sɩ diin a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yɩ a fʊ yuor pɔ ɛ, na sɩ tʊ̃ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ a fʊ yuor pɔ ɛ?

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: A daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ɩn 'yɛr kʊ ba, n ba bɔ̃ɔ yɩ ɛ, yɩ tɔɔ chen be, yɩn yelbe-ɩrbɛ.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Saa wa naa, ka a kʊ̃ɔ yɩ yaar jilʊ̃ a yir ka a sɛsɛbɛ fu ŋme a yir tɩchɛ waa lo ɛ, bojũu wʊ ara na kʊsɩr ju.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
text: Yesu na wa 'yɛr a 'yɛrbie anyãna baar nɛɛ 'maan a nɩyɔɔ nɩ a wʊ wiilfʊ,

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: bojũu wʊ wile ban nɩ kpɛ̃ɔ gar a ba nɛbinãa wiwiilbe.

ref: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
text: tɩchɛ a bala na so a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ, wʊn lɔb ban bar yõo a jilibe pɔ, be lɛ ban kõnõ ɔɔrɔ nɩ nyɩmɛ.”

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: A tẽe 'lɔ nɩbɛ wa gere yɩ, yɩ yi a be fɔɔ chen tẽyuo; yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩ kʊ yɔ a Isɩral tẽn bar ɛ, tɩchɛ ka a Nɩsaal Bie wa ɛ.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: “Jãjãna ba gar a wʊ wiwile ɛ bɩɩ ka gbãgbaa gar a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ɛ.

ref: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
text: A sɛɛn ɩka a jãjãna wʊ nyɛtãan a wʊ wiwile, ka a gbãgbaa mɩ nyɛtãan a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ. Ɩ ba bʊɔlɔ na a yir sʊɔ Beeljebul, yodɛɛr buor lɛ ban pãa bʊɔlɔ a yir dẽme!

ref: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
text: Yɩ ta joro a bala na kʊ a ãgãn tɩ kʊ tʊ̃ɔ kʊ a sɩɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ joro a 'lɔ nɩ na tʊ̃ɔ kʊ a sɩɛ nɩ a ãgãn a bũu dagel 'lɔ pɔ.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Ba ma dãn tiitasɩ ayi kobsɩ ayi. Tɩchɛ ãsʊɔ kʊ lo tẽe ɛ, ala baa ɩ a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn bɔfʊ lɛ ɛ.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Nãaŋmɩn sɔr a yɩ jukɔɔl a jaa bɔ̃ɔ.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: A lɛ jũu, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, yɩ tara na tɔ̃nɔ̃ gar a tiitasɩ.

ref: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
text: “Nɩrɛ jaa na wa 'yɛr ɩka wʊ bɔ̃ɔ ma naa a nɩbɛ niem, 'lɔ lɛ n mɩ na 'yɛr ɩka n bɔ̃ɔ naa a n Sãa na be a saaju niem,

ref: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
text: tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na chiir ma a nɩbɛ niem, 'lɔ lɛ n mɩ na chiir a n Sãa na be a saaju niem.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Yɩ ta tɩɛr ɩka nyã'maar lɛ n waan a wɛr pɔ ɛ; n ba waan nyã'maar ɛ tɩchɛ ootaa lɛ n waan.

ref: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
text: N wan ɩka n wa vɛ̃ ka ‘Bie nɩ sãa ootaa, ka bipɔɔ oo a ma, pɔɔ na oo naa a wʊ bidaba pɔɔ ka a bidaba pɔɔ mɩ oo a wʊ sɩrɛ ma.

ref: Your worst enemies will be in your own family.
text: Yir dẽme na lɩɛb ɩ dɔ̃dẽme.’ (Mika

ref: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: “Nɩrɛ jaa na nʊ̃ a wʊ sãa bɩɩ a wʊ ma gar ma wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a n poturo ɛ, nɩrɛ jaa mɩ na nʊ̃ a wʊ bie bɩɩ bipɔɔ gar ma, wʊ kʊ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a n poturo ɛ.

ref: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na ba de a wʊ daa tuo turo nɩ ma ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a n poturo ɛ.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Bala na bʊɔrɔ a ba nyɛvʊr na bɔr wʊ naa tɩchɛ bala mɩ na bɔr a ba nyɛvʊr a maa jũu na nyɛ wʊ naa.

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na sɔɔ de a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ wʊn ɩ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ jũu na nyɛn a wʊ sãyar. Nɩrɛ jaa mɩ na sɔɔ de nɩrɛ na ɩ nɩtor wʊn ɩ nɩtor jũu na nyɛn a nɩtor sãyar.

ref: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
text: “Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na sɔɔ de yɩ, sɔɔn de ma, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa mɩ na sɔɔ de ma sɔɔn de a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma.

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na kʊ a bʊ̃bile banyãna ãsʊɔ kɔ̃'maar nɩ ŋmele wʊ̃n ɩ a n poturbo jũu, yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a nɩnyɩnɛ kʊ̃ faa a wʊ sãyar ɛ.”

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Ban wa chen baar, Yesu piel 'yɛrɛ a Jɔɔn 'yɛr kʊrɔ a nɩyɔɔ, “Bo lɛ yɩ dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ ɩka yɩ tɩ kaa? A jɩmur a sɛsɛbɛ na dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ bɩɩ?

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: A Jɔɔn na wa, waa dire ɛ, na waa lɛ nyuro go ɛ, ka ba 'yɛr ɩka sɩdɛɛr lɛ wʊ ɩ.

ref: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
text: Jɔɔn na be a nɩpɔɔ die pɔ, tɩ wa wõ lɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na maala, wʊ tʊ̃n a wʊ poturbo bamɩne

ref: Jesus answered, “Go and tell John what you have heard and seen.
text: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ lɩɛb kul tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a Jɔɔn, a lɛ yɩn wõ tɩ nyɛ.

ref: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
text: Jɔ̃n nyɛrɛ naa, ka a gbɛrsɩ mɩ chiine, ka kɔ̃kɔ̃n mɩ sa, ka wõn mɩ wõnõ jaa chɛchɛ, ka kũusɩ jɛɔ, ka 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ mɩ ta a nɔ̃n dẽme.

ref: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
text: Pʊpɛl sʊɔ lɛ a 'lɔ na ba baala ma ɛ.”

ref: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
text: Kai, ka bo lɛ yɩ chen ɩka yɩ tɩ kaa? Daba na su bʊ̃vɩɛl lɛ yɩ chen ɩka yɩ tɩ kaa bɩɩ? Nyɛ a bala na ma su bʊ̃vɩɛl ɛ, nãmɩne yie lɛ ba ma be.

ref: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
text: Tɩchɛ bo lɛ yɩ chen ɩka yɩ tɩ kaa? Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ bɩɩ? Ɔ̃ɔ, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka wʊ gar Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.

ref: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka bala jaa pɔɔ na dɔɔ ka a wɛr pɔ, ba ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ gar a Jɔɔn kɔ̃suor ɛ, tɩchɛ 'lɔ mɩ na ɩ a pʊʊr a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ gar wʊ naa.

ref: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
text: An yi a Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suoro sɔ̃ɔ wa tɩ tãn a dɩna a Nãaŋmɩn Nãalʊ̃ʊ nʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ naa ka a kpãɛn dẽme sɔ̃ɔ wʊ.

ref: And if you believe them, John is Elijah, the prophet you are waiting for.
text: Ala ɩ yɩn sɔɔn de, a anyãna 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Elaija nɩ naa lɩɛb wa.

ref: You people are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other,
text: “Tɩchɛ bo lɛ ɩn de a dɩna nɩbɛ man? Ba nyɛntãan bibiir na ma jãnɛ a ba dãɛn jiir na ba bʊɔlɔ taa,

ref: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
text: sɩ pɛɛl a ɩlɛ tɩ yaa sɛɛrɛ bãnɛ ɛ, na sɩ kõnõ kõyiel yaa ŋmara nɩbikʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Tɩchɛ ka a Nɩsaal Bie wa na wʊ ma dire tɩ nyuro ka ba 'yɛr ɩka di dire nɩ dãburo lɛ wʊ ɩ, na wʊ ɩ a lõpodierbe nɩ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ baraa. A ma wiil a lɛ Nãaŋmɩn na tor, bala wa nyɛ lɛ a wʊ nɩbɛ na kpɩɛrɛ nɩ yɛ̃.”

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
text: Yesu pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔn naa a tẽn bala nɩbɛ a be a wʊ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ na tʊ̃, tɩchɛ ka baa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ.

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
text: A yɩn Kapenahum dẽme, ban 'mɔɔ yɩn don saaju bɩɩ? Kai, ban suun yɩn a kpĩisɩ tẽe. Ala ɩ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ na tʊ̃ a yɩ pɔ mɩ ta tʊ̃n a Sodom pɔ, a tẽe anyãna naa chɛnɛ na wa tɔ dɩna.

ref: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
text: Tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar Nãaŋmɩn na kãan Sodom nɩbɛ nɩbaal gar a yɩn.”

ref: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.
text: A daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ Yesu 'yɛr naa, “N dãn a n Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a saaju nɩ a tẽe wʊ jaa Soro, bojũu fʊ sɔɔl a yele anyãna a yɛ̃n dẽme sɛ̃ tɩ ir wiil bibiir.

ref: Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
text: Ɔ̃ɔ, n Sãa lɛ lɛ ɩ a fʊ pʊpɛl ka fʊ maal.

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: “N Sãa den a yele a jaa kʊ, nɩrɛ jaa ba bɔ̃ɔ a Bie ɛ, ka a Sãa ba lɛ ɛ, nɩrɛ jaa mɩ ba bɔ̃ɔ a Sãa ɛ, ka a Bie ba lɛ ɛ, nɩ a bala a Nãaŋmɩn Bie na ir, na wiil ban a Sãa.

ref: If you are tired from carrying heavy burdens, come to me and I will give you rest.
text: “Yɩ wa a n sɛ̃, yɩn banyã na bal na yɩ tuo tuor ka a wõnõ yɩ, ɩn too yɩn a yɩ pɛ̃n.

ref: Take the yoke I give you. Put it on your shoulders and learn from me. I am gentle and humble, and you will find rest.
text: Yɩ de a n daa tuo na yɩ jãn yi a n sɛ̃, bojũu n ɩn jomm sʊɔ, naa siir a n mãɛ a n nyãa pɔ, yɩn nyɛn pɛ̃nfʊ kʊ a yɩ sɩɩr.

ref: This yoke is easy to bear, and this burden is light.
text: Bojũu a n daa ɩn kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃, ka yɩn tʊ̃ɔ tuo a tuor ala ɩn na kʊ yɩ.”

ref: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
text: A Faraseemɩne na wa nyɛ a lɛ, ba sʊʊr a Yesu, “Fʊ nyɛn lɛ na ba tu a nɛbinãa a fʊ poturbo na ɩrɛ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar ɔ?”

ref: The crowds were so amazed they asked, “Could Jesus be the Son of David?”
text: Nɛɛr 'maan a nɩyɔɔ ka ba sʊrɔ taa ɩka, kaa 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Nãa David Bie bɩɩ?

ref: But when I force out demons by the power of God's Spirit, it proves that God's kingdom has already come to you.
text: Ala ɩ Satan diin Satan kʊ yi na wʊ ŋma dẽdẽme ŋmɛ lɛ a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ na ɩ ara? Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ lɛ kʊ ma a kpɛ̃ɔ ka dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yire wiil ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ ta yɩ naa.

ref: Haven't you read in the Law of Moses that the priests are allowed to work in the temple on the Sabbath? But no one says they are guilty of breaking the law of the Sabbath.
text: Bɩɩ yaa kar a Mosesɩ nɛbinãa pɔ lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlorbo a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar a puorfʊ yir pɔ na ma tʊ̃ tɩ na baa bʊɔlɔ a lɛ bier ɛ?

ref: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,
text: Yesu yin a be chen a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir pɔ,

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
text: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a daba 'lɔ nũu na kpi, “Tur a fʊ nũu.” A wʊ tur ka a nũu wʊ jaa sa kaara a nũu nyã sʊɔ kaar.

ref: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
text: Yesu na wa bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban piel guoro ɩka ba ɩ wʊ, wʊ yin a jie 'lɔ ka nɩyɔɔ jaa turo wʊ. Wʊ sãan a bala an ɔɔrɔ.

ref: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba ɩka ba ta 'yɛr a wʊ yele kʊ nɩrɛ jaa ɛ.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Wʊ maal a nɩtɔ̃, ka a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr tu a 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Isaiya na ɩ yelmãɛ,

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “A n tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a nyã ɩn kaa ir, a 'lɔ n na nʊ̃, a 'lɔ a n pʊɔ na pɛlʊ. Ɩn yɔ̃ɔ wʊn a n Sɩɛ, ka wʊ mʊɔl a n 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ yele kʊ a nɩbɛ ba jaa.

ref: If you are not on my side, you are against me. If you don't gather in the harvest with me, you scatter it.
text: “Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba be a n sɛ̃ ɛ, tara man nɩ 'yɛr, nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba pʊɔ ma a sɩ kaal lɔ̃ɔ taa ɛ wʊ ma yɛ̃yɛl bar.

ref: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.
text: Ba waan na daba na ɩ jɔ̃ɔ tɩ ɩ wõo na tara sɩdɛbɛ Yesu sɛ̃. Wʊ sãn a daba baal, ka wʊ nyɛrɛ tɩ lɛ 'yɛrɛ jaa chɛchɛ.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Yesu na bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban tɩɛrɛ, a wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Tẽe 'lɔ na ŋma dẽdẽme jɛɛrɛ taa kʊ kor ɛ. Tẽe 'lɔ bɩɩ yir kpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ na ŋma dẽdẽme jɛɛrɛ taa kʊ ara ɛ.

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Ala ɩ Beeljebul lɛ kʊ ma a kpɛ̃ɔ ka dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ, tɩ a bala na ɩ a yɩ poturbo, bo lɛ ba tara dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ? A lɛ jũu bala lɛ na ɩ a yɩ 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ.

ref: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when the demon doesn't find a place,
text: “Sɩdɛɛr wa yɩ nɩrɛ pɔ, wʊ ma chen dãlpʊʊr pɔ tɩ bʊɔrɔ pɛ̃nfʊ jie 'lɔ baa wa nyɛ ɛ,

ref: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and the person ends up in worse shape than before. That's how it will be with you evil people of today. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Mark
text: ka wʊ chen tɩ bʊɔl sɩdɛbɛ ayopõi na tara a putuo gar wʊ ka ba tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a be. Ka a nɩrɛ nyã pãa ɩ dɛɛr gar a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ be. Lɛ lɛ an kaara a yɩn putudẽme a dɩna.”

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
text: “Ala ɩ tɩɛ vɩɛlɛ na wʊ ma wɔ̃n wɔ̃vɩɛl, ala ɩ tɩɛ ɩn tɩdɛɛr wʊ ma wɔ̃n wɔ̃dɛbɛ. Tɩɛ jaa a wʊ wɔ̃mɔ̃ lɛ ba ma de bɔ̃ɔn wʊ.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Yɩn waa bibiir! Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩn tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr 'yɛr vɩɛlsɩ ala ɩ yɩ ɩn yelbe-ɩrbɛ? A ala na sɛɛ a pʊɔ lɛ a nɛɛ ma 'yɛr.

ref: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
text: Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar, nɩrɛ jaa na man 'yɛrdɛɛr 'lɔ jaa bʊrɔ na dɔ̃ɔ yi a wʊ nɛɛ pɔ.

ref: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
text: A fʊ 'yɛrbie lɛ na wiil a lɛ fʊn be, naa wiil a fʊ sɔ̃ɔnaa.”

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Be lɛ ka a nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne bamɩne 'yɛr, “Wiwile, sɩ bʊɔrɔ na ɩka sɩ nyɛ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ na ɩ jãnɛ a fʊ jie.”

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “A yɩn banyãna na ɩ a putudẽme nɩ sɛ̃sɛ̃nbɛ tɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka n tʊ̃ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ka a na ɩ jãnɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ ãsʊɔ jaa ɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Jona jãnɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
text: Nɩtãa lɛ a Jona na dɔ̃ɔ gã a jãnele pʊɔm ber ata nɩ tãsɔɔ gbulo ata lɛ mɩ lɛ a Maa Nɩsaal Bie mɩ na gã a yaa pɔ ber ata nɩ tãsɔɔ gbulo ata.

ref: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
text: A 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar Ninive nɩbɛ na yin 'yɔ̃ɔ a yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ 'yɛr, bojũu ba lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr a Jona na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ ba, tɩchɛ yɩ kaa, a 'lɔ na gar a Jona ben ka.

ref: The Queen of the South will also stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something much greater than Solomon. Return of an Evil Spirit (Luke

text: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ tara kãnyir, maa lɛ, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ.”

ref: Peter replied, “Lord, if it really is you, tell me to come to you on the water.”
text: Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “N Soro, ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ vɛ̃ ka chiine a kʊ̃ɔ ju wa a fʊ sɛ̃.”

ref: At once, Jesus reached out his hand. He helped Peter up and said, “You surely don't have much faith. Why do you doubt?”
text: Ajienaa lɛ, a Yesu tur a wʊ nũu nyɔɔ wʊ tɩ 'yɛr, “Fʊ̃ʊ sɔɔfʊ na ba jã bon ɛ, bo so ka fʊ baala?”

ref: When Jesus and Peter got into the boat, the wind died down.
text: Ban wa kpɛ a gboro pɔ baar ka a sɛsɛbɛ ŋmaa jaa jomm.

ref: The men in the boat worshiped Jesus and said, “You really are the Son of God!” Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Mark
text: A bala na be a gboro pɔ gbur dumo puor wʊ tɩ 'yɛrɛ, “Yelmãɛ, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and came to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Ban wa gɔ̃ɔ a man baar ba chen a Genesaretɩ wɛr pɔ.

ref: The people found out he was there, and they sent word to everyone who lived in this part of the country. So they brought all the sick people to Jesus.
text: A tẽe 'lɔ dẽme na wa wõ a wʊ yele ba 'yɛr lʊɔr a paal 'lɔ wʊ jaa na ba waan bala jaa an ɔɔrɔ,

ref: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: na ba wa sʊɔrɔ ɩka ba sɩɩr a wʊ fuu nɛɛ, a bala jaa na sɩɩr a fuu nɛɛ sa naa.

ref: Jesus answered, “Every plant that my Father in heaven did not plant will be pulled up by the roots.
text: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, “Tɩɛ 'lɔ jaa a n Sãa na be a saaju na ba sɛl ɛ, ɩn vʊ̃ɔ wʊn bar.

ref: “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? They don't even wash their hands before they eat.”
text: “Ŋmɩnɛ a fʊ poturbo ba turo a sɩ sãakpãmɩne wiilfʊ ɛ? Ba maa pɛɛ a ba nuur tɩ di ɛ.”

ref: Jesus answered: Why do you disobey God and follow your own teaching?
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Ŋmɩnɛ ka yɩ sɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nɛbinsɩ tɩ na yɩ turo a yɩ mãɛ wiilfʊ?

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
text: Tɩchɛ yɩn 'yɛr ɩka nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na 'yɛr a wʊ sãa bɩɩ ma ɩka, sʊ̃ʊfʊ 'lɔ jaa ɩn tara naa kʊ fʊ, n den kʊ Nãaŋmɩn.

ref: Is this any way to show respect to your parents? You ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching.
text: Tɩchɛ na waa de a wʊ bʊ̃tarsɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ a a wʊ sãa ɛ, yɩn ɩ a lɛ, yɩ sɔ̃ɔn Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ, tɩ na yɩ turo a yɩ mãɛ binfʊ.

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.” What Really Makes People Unclean (Mark
text: A ba puorfʊ ba tara tɔ̃nɔ̃ kʊ ma ɛ, nɩsaal wiilfʊ lɛ ba wile.’ ” (Isaiya

ref: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
text: Yɩ bar ba, jɔ̃n lɛ tɔɔrɔ jɔ̃n. Jɔ̃ɔ wa tɔɔrɔ a wʊ jɔ̃ɔ tɔ sʊɔ ba jaa ma lon bɔɔ pɔ.”

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Tɩchɛ a Yesu ba 'yɛr 'yɛr jaa kʊ wʊ ɛ. Ka a wʊ poturbo wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Dii wʊ bar, wʊ pɔɔrɔ sɩn gɔ̃mɔ̃.”

ref: The food you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
text: A ba ɩ bon 'lɔ na ma tu a nɩsaal nɛɛ pɔ kpɛ lɛ ma sɔ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ bon 'lɔ na ma yɩ a nɩsaal nɛɛ pɔ lɛ ma sɔ̃ɔ wʊ.”

ref: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
text: A poturbo wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Fʊ bɔ̃ɔn naa an kpɛ a Faraseemɩne ban wõ a 'yɛr nyã fʊn 'yɛr ɔ?”

ref: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
text: Tɩchɛ ka Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Wiil a sʊkpa nyã par kʊ sɩ.”

ref: Jesus then said: Don't any of you know by now what I am talking about?
text: Be lɛ wʊ 'yɛr, “A yɩn mɩ na ɩ a n poturbo ba bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ?

ref: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?
text: Yaa bɔ̃ɔ ka bon 'lɔ jaa na tu a nɛɛ pɔ kpɛ a pʊɔ pɔ ma tun a par bɔɔ pɔ yi bɩɩ?

ref: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
text: Tɩchɛ bon 'lɔ na ma yi a nɛɛ pɔ, yin a tɩɛr pɔ, anyãna lɛ ma sɔ̃ɔ a nɩsaal.

ref: Out of your heart come evil thoughts, murder, unfaithfulness in marriage, vulgar deeds, stealing, telling lies, and insulting others.
text: A nɩsaal tɩɛr pɔ lɛ a tɩɛr dɛbɛ ma yi, nɩrɛ kʊfʊ, pɔɔdeyele, sɛ̃sɛ̃yele, nãnyiyele, jir dãasɩɛ, nɩ a gagaryele ma be.

ref: These are what make you unclean. Eating without washing your hands will not make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Mark
text: A anyãna lɛ a bonsɩ ala na ma sɔ̃ɔ a nɩsaal tɩchɛ nɩrɛ na din nũdɛbɛ ba sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ ɛ.”

ref: Jesus said, “I was sent only to the people of Israel! They are like a flock of lost sheep.”
text: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, “A piir ala na bɔr a Isɩral nɩbɛ pɔ tɛɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ ba tʊ̃ ma.”

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: A nɩyɔɔ tara na a nɩbɛ gbɛɛ na kpi kpi, nɩ jɔ̃n, nɩ a gbɛrsɩ, nɩ a wõn 'matãan baalsɩ yɔɔ waan a wʊ sɛ̃. Ba wa bin ban a wʊ niem a wʊ sãa a ba jaa baalsɩ.

ref: Jesus left and went to the territory near the towns of Tyre and Sidon.
text: Yesu yin a be chen a Tire paal nɩ Sidon paal pɔ.

ref: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
text: Be lɛ a pɔɔ na yi a Kanaan paal pɔ yi piel ŋmiere chɛlsɩ, “N Soro kaa a n nɩbaal David Bie, a sɩdɛɛr lɛ tara a n bipɔɔ.”

ref: The woman came closer. Then she knelt down and begged, “Please help me, Lord!”
text: Tɩchɛ ka a pɔɔ wa gbur dumo a wʊ niem 'yɛr, “N Soro, sʊ̃ʊ ma.”

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “A ba sɛɛ ɩka fʊ de bibiir bʊ̃diir lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ baar ɛ.”

ref: “Lord, this is true,” the woman said, “but even puppies get the crumbs that fall from their owner's table.”
text: Ka a pɔɔ 'yɛr, “Ɔ̃ɔ, n Soro, a baar gba ma dire na a bʊ̃di pʊr pʊr ala na ma ŋmara loro a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ dakɔɔ pĩlem.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
text: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a pɔɔ, “Pɔɔ, a fʊ sɔɔfʊ bɛrma naa! A ɩ kʊ fʊ a lɛ fʊn bʊɔrɔ.” A wʊ bipɔɔ sa naa a dẽdẽe daa 'lɔ̃nɔ̃.

ref: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
text: Yesu na wa yi a be, wʊ den a Galilee man yal kõkoor 'lana, na wʊ tɩ do tɔ̃ɔ ju jãnɛ.

ref: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
text: Nɛɛr 'maan a nɩbɛ ban wõ ka a wõn 'yɛrɛ, ka a gbɛrsɩ sa chiine ka a bala a gbɛɛ nɩ na kpi kpi mɩ sa ka a jɔ̃n mɩ nyɛrɛ. Ka ba dãna a Isɩral nɩbɛ Nãaŋmɩn.

ref: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
text: Yesu bʊɔl a wʊ poturbo wa 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Nɩbaal kpɛ ma naa nɩ a nɩyɔɔ banyãna bojũu ba ben a n sɛ̃ ber ata tɩ ba di bon jaa ɛ, n ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka n vɛ̃ ka ba lɩɛb chiine kɔ̃ ɛ, amɩne ban wa lon sɔr pɔ.”

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Ka a wʊ poturbo 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Nyɛ lɛ sɩn nyɛ paanʊ̃ɔ a dãlpʊɔ nyã pɔ tʊ̃ɔ kãan a nɩyɔɔ banyãna?”

ref: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
text: Ka Yesu sʊʊr ba, “Paanʊ̃ɔ aŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tara?” Ka ba 'yɛr, “Ayopõi nɩ jãmɛ bãlãa.”

ref: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
text: Wʊ vɛ̃n ka a nɩbɛ jãnɛ tẽe.

ref: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
text: Ka wʊ pãa de a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ ayopõi nɩ a jãbãlãa ala na wʊ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, tɩ na wʊ ŋma de kʊ a wʊ poturbo, ka ba de põ a nɩbɛ.

ref: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
text: Ba jaa din tɩɩ, ka a poturbo wob a bʊ̃ŋmaar 'yɔ̃ɔ pɛr ayopõi.

ref: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
text: Wʊn wa vɛ̃ ka a nɩbɛ yaar baar wʊ kpɛ̃n a gboro na wʊ chen Magadan paal pɔ.

ref: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
text: Bo lɛ so ka yaa tɩɛr bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a ba ɩ paanʊ̃ɔ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ ɛ? Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Saduseemɩne dãbɩl yele pɔ.”

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
text: A Yesu na wa kpɛ a Sisariya Filipai paal pɔ, wʊ sʊʊr a wʊ poturbo, “An lɛ a nɩbɛ 'yɛr ɩka a maa Nɩsaal Bie ɩ naa?”

ref: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
text: Be lɛ ba pãa tɩɛr bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a ba ɩ a paanʊ̃ɔ dãbɩl lɛ ɛ tɩchɛ ba bɔ̃ɔn a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Saduseemɩne wiilfʊ.

ref: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
text: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Ala wa ɩ jãanʊɔr lɛ, yɩ ma 'yɛr ɩka a wɛr chaar naa bojũu a saaju mʊ̃ɔ naa.

ref: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
text: Ala wa ɩ bibio lɛ, yɩ ma 'yɛr ɩka saa na wan a dɩna, a julõjuur sɔb naa ka saa koro. Yɩ ma kãan a saaju tɩ 'yɛr ɩka saa na wan bɩɩ wʊ kʊ̃ wa ɛ, yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a tẽe nɩ a saaju 'yɛr ma 'yɛr, ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a jãnsɩ a dɩna ɛ?

ref: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
text: Ka ba 'yɛr, “Bamɩne 'yɛr ɩka Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suoro, ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛr ɩka Elaija lɛ, ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛr ɩka Jeremiya bɩɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ãsʊɔ lɛ.”

ref: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
text: An piel a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a Yesu piel 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ ba ɩka wʊn chen Jerusalɛm tɩ di dɔɔyɛ a nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɛɛ wiwiilbe nuur pɔ, ka ba kʊ wʊ, ka a ber ata daar a wʊ lɛ ir ɩ vʊr.

ref: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
text: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Saduseemɩne dãbɩl pɔ.”

ref: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
text: Ba piel mana kʊrɔ taa, “Sɩn ba waan paanʊ̃ɔ jaa lɛ so.”

ref: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
text: A maa Nɩsaal Bie na waan naa Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr nɩ a wʊ malakasɩ wa yab nɩrɛ jaa bon 'lɔ wʊn maal.

ref: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
text: Maa lɛ 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ fʊ, a fʊ̃ʊ Pita, a par lɛ kʊsɩr pie, a fʊ 'yɛr nyãna pɔ lɛ, ɩn tu bʊɔl nɩbɛ a ba wa sɔɔ de ma ka a kpĩi tẽe kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ba ɛ.

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
text: Tɩchɛ a wʊ sʊʊr ba, “An lɛ yɩn 'yɛr ɩka n ɩ naa?”

ref: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
text: Ka Simon Pita 'yɛr, “Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Nɩ-iraa, a Nãaŋmɩn Vʊr Bie.”

ref: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
text: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Fʊ ju nʊ̃mɔ̃ na Simon, Jona bie! A ba ɩ nɩsaal nɩ nɩrɛ lɛ ir wiil fʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a n Sãa na be a saaju lɛ.

ref: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: Ɩn kʊ fʊn a saaju nãalʊ̃ʊ pãn bie, bon 'lɔ jaa fʊn lũ ka a wɛr pɔ, a mɩn lũn a be a saaju, bon 'lɔ jaa fʊn for bar ka a wɛr pɔ, a mɩn for bar a be a saaju.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
text: Ka wʊ pãa kpãkpãan a wʊ poturbo ɩka ba taa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ nɩrɛ jaa ɩka 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa ɛ.

ref: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
text: Ka Pita tɔɔ wʊ yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ na wʊ tɩ piel 'yɛrɛ, “N Soro, Nãaŋmɩn kʊ sɔɔ ka a yele nyã ɩ fʊ ɛ.”

ref: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Tɩchɛ a Yesu lɩɛb 'yɛr kʊ a Pita, “Tɔɔ lan ka tɩ chɛ ma. Fʊ ɩn Satan, na fʊ ɩ gbɛr ŋme bon kʊ ma. Faa tara Nãaŋmɩn bonsɩ tɩɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩsaal bonsɩ tɩɛr lɛ fʊ tara.”

ref: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Ka a Yesu pãa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, “Ala ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa bʊɔrɔ na ɩka wʊ ɩ a n poturo, wʊ 'laar a wʊ mãɛ tɩ 'mɔɔ a wʊ daa tuo turo nɩ ma.

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
text: A nɩrɛ 'lɔ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ faa a wʊ nyɛvʊr na bɔr wʊ naa, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na bɔr a wʊ nyɛvʊr a maa jũu, na nyɛ wʊ naa.

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
text: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ tara naa a wɛr ju bonsɩ a jaa, tɩ bɔr a wʊ nyɛvʊr, bo tʊ̃nɔ̃ lɛ wʊn nyɛ? Bɩɩ bo lɛ wʊn de tɛɛn a wʊ nyɛvʊr?

ref: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a yɩ bamɩne na ara a ka kʊ̃ kpi ɛ, tɩ na yɩ nyɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie na waara a n nãa difʊ jie.”

ref: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
text: Wʊ chil wʊ mãɛ a ba niem, ka a wʊ nie nyɩlɛ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ kaar, ka a fuu pɛl nyɩlɛ bũu kaar.

ref: Then the disciples understood Jesus was talking to them about John the Baptist. Jesus Heals a Boy (Mark
text: Be lɛ a poturbo pãa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suoro 'yɛr lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ.

ref: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
text: A ber ayʊɔb daar, Yesu tara na Pita nɩ Jemesɩ nɩ a wʊ yɛbɛ Jɔɔn don tɔ̃wõo ju a ba tɛɛ.

ref: All at once Moses and Elijah were there talking with Jesus.
text: Mosesɩ nɩ Elaija mɩ wa dũun yɩ ara a wʊ sɛ̃, ka ba wa 'mataa 'yɛrɛ.

ref: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
text: Pita 'yɛr kʊ Yesu, “N Soro, a vɩla na ka sɩn kpɩɛrɛ a ka. Ala ɩ fʊ sɔɔ naa, ɩn 'yɔ̃ɔn sɛɛr ata a ka na, ka fʊ so a ãsʊɔ, ka Mosesɩ mɩ so a ãsʊɔ, ka Elaija mɩ so a ãsʊɔ.”

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Wʊn chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ ka a julõjupɩla wa pɔɔ ba naa, ka Nãaŋmɩn kɔkɔr 'yɛr be a julõjuur pɔ, “Nyã lɛ a n Binʊ̃nãa, a n pʊɔ pɛl wʊ naa, yɩ chɛlɛ a wʊ 'yɛr.”

ref: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
text: A poturbo na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃ dãbãɛ kpɛ ba naa ka ba lo kpa tẽe.

ref: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
text: Tɩchɛ ka Yesu wa sɩɩr ba tɩ 'yɛrɛ, “Yɩ ir, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ.”

ref: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
text: Ban wa dʊɔ kaa, baa nyɛ nɩrɛ jaa ka a Yesu tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

ref: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
text: Ka a wʊ poturbo sʊʊr wʊ, “Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a ɩ ka a nɛɛ wiwiilbe 'yɛr ɩka Elaija lɛ na de nie wa?”

ref: Jesus told them, “Elijah certainly will come and get everything ready.
text: Ka Yesu 'yɛr, “Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, ɩka Elaija wa naa wa chɔɔr a bonsɩ a jaa.

text: Ka ba sɔɔ chen. Tɩ wʊ lɛ yɩ go a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa vɩrʊ̃ gar ju sãlsɔɔ nɩ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa suro a tɩɩr pɔ na wʊ tɩ bɔ a nɩbɛ bamɩne mɩ.

ref: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
text: A jãanɔ'maar sɔ̃ɔ, wʊ lɛ yin tɩ nyɛ bamɩne mɩ na chɛnɛ jɛ̃ pɔrɔ jaa. Ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, ‘Bo lɛ so a dɩna yɩn jãnɛ ba maala bon jaa ɛ?’

ref: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
text: A ba 'yɛr, ‘Nɩrɛ jaa ba sʊɔr sɩ kɔb ɛ.’ Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, ‘Yɩ wa a sɩ chen a n puo pɔ a yɩ tɩ kɔ.’

ref: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
text: “Jãanʊɔr na wa ta, a puo sʊɔ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kaara a wʊ puo, ‘Bʊɔl a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ kʊ ba a ba libie, de nie piel a bala na bɩɛr wa tɩ tãn a bala na dɔ̃ɔ nie wa.’

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: “A bala na wa bɩɛr wa ãsʊɔ jaa den a bɩbir gbul libir.

ref: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
text: Tɩchɛ ban wa de a lɛ baar, ba vʊ̃n nɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a puo sʊɔ,

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: na ba gɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a puo sʊɔ 'yɛrɛ, ‘A nɩbɛ banyãna wa bɩɛr wa tɩ fʊ yab sɩ jãtaa, nɩ sɩn na ŋmaa ir nɩ ɔɔr wa tʊ̃ lɛ̃ɛɛ ka mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ ŋme sɩ tɩ mʊrʊ̃.’

ref: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
text: “Ka wʊ lɩɛb sʊʊr a ba ãsʊɔ, ‘N badaba, n ba ɩ fʊ dɛɛr ɛ, a ba ɩ yɩn lɛ sɔɔ ɩka yɩn den a bɩbir gbul libir ɛ?

ref: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
text: De a fʊ libir chiine. Maa lɛ nʊ̃ naa kʊ a puor dẽme nɩtãa a lɛ ɩn kʊ yɩ.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: N ba tara sɔr na de a n libie ɩn a lɛ ɩn bʊɔrɔ bɩɩ? Bɩɩ fʊ tara nyuur nɩ a n maal vɩla?’

ref: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
text: “A lɛ jũu, a puor dẽme na wa ben niem ka a nie dẽme mɩ wa be a puor.”

ref: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
text: Yesu ara na tɩ bʊɔl ba sʊrɔ, “Bo lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka n maal kʊ yɩ?”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Nɩbaal kpɛ̃n a Yesu, ka wʊ sɩɩr a ba nɩbie, ka ba nyɛrɛ na ba turo wʊ.

ref: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Ka a nɩyɔɔ 'yɛr ɩka ba bar gɔ̃mɔ̃ tɩchɛ ka ba chɛnɛ gboolo, “Sɩ Soro, David Bie wooo ɩ, kaa a sɩ nɩbaal.”

ref: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
text: Yesu na wa pãa duoro a Jerusalɛm, wʊ tara na a poturbo pie nɩ ayi yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ na wʊ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba,

ref: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
text: Ban de man kʊ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ka ba la ma, tʊrɔ ma, chɩrɛ nataar 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma, na ba ge ma, tɩ kʊ ma, ka a ber ata daar ka n ir ɩ vʊr go.”

ref: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
text: Be lɛ ka Jebedee pɔɔ tara a wʊ bibiir ayi waan a wʊ sɛ̃ wa lo gbur dumo a wʊ niem na wʊ sʊɔrɔ bon kɔ̃ɔ.

ref: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
text: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr wʊ, “Bo lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ?” Ka wʊ 'yɛr, “Vɛ̃ ka a n bibiir banyã ayi ka ãsʊɔ jãnɛ a fʊ nũsʊ̃ɔ ka ãsʊɔ mɩ jãnɛ a fʊ gʊbaa jie a fʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “Faa bɔ̃ɔ a bon 'lɔ fʊn sʊɔrɔ ɛ. Ban tʊ̃ɔ nyũn a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele 'lɔ ɩn na nyũn ɔ?” Ka ba 'yɛr, “Sɩn tʊ̃ɔ naa.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩn tʊ̃ɔ nyũn a n ŋmele tɩchɛ maa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr a buor sʊɔ na jãnɛ a n nũsʊ̃ɔ bɩɩ a n gʊbaa jie ɛ, ka a bala a n Sãa na chɔɔr kʊ tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.”

ref: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
text: A poturbo pie bala na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃, ba nyɛn naa a yɛɛr ayi bala suur.

ref: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.
text: Ka a Yesu bʊɔl ba waan wa 'yɛr, “Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn na ɩka a bala na kaara a nɩbɛ bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ lɛ kaara ba bon jaa pɔ, ka a ba nɩbɛrɛ tʊ̃ɔ ba.

ref: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.
text: A kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ be a lɛ a yɩ pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ a yɩ kpɛ̃ɛ, wʊ ɩ a yɩ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃.

ref: And if you want to be first, you must be the slave of the rest.

ref: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
text: Nɩtãa lɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie na ba wa ɩka ba tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ ma ɛ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ ɩ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ na wʊ de a n nyɛvʊr faan nɩyɔɔ yelbier bar.”

ref: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
text: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo na wa yire a Jeriko nɩyɔɔ turo wʊ naa.

text: Ka a jɔ̃n ayi jãnɛ a sɔr kõkoor. Ban wa wõ ɩka Yesu lɛ tala, ka ba gboolo, “Sɩ Soro, David Bie wooo ɩ, kaa a sɩ nɩbaal.”

ref: They answered, “Lord, we want to see!”
text: Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Sɩ Soro, sɩ bʊɔrɔ na ɩka sɩ nyɛrɛ.”

ref: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tɩɛr? Daba lɛ tara bibiir ayi, na wʊ chen tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a dɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ, ‘N Bie chen tɩ tʊ̃ a n puo tʊ̃mɔ̃ a dɩna.’

ref: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
text: “Tɩchɛ a pokaarba na wa nyɛ a bie, ba 'yɛr kʊ taa, ‘Nyã lɛ ɩ a bʊ̃dire, yɩ ɩ a sɩ kʊ wʊ tɩ de a bonsɩ.’

ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
text: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ wa sʊʊr yɩ naa, ‘Ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ foro a bʊ̃ɔ?’ Yɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘A Sɩ Soro lɛ bʊɔrɔ yaa.’ Wʊn bar sɔr ka yɩ foraa waan fɔɔ jaa.”

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: A yele anyã ɩ na ka a na ɩ yelmãɛ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr na wʊ 'yɛrɛ.

ref: “Announce to the people of Jerusalem: ‘Your king is coming to you! He is humble and rides on a donkey. He comes on the colt of a donkey.’ ”
text: “'Yɛr kʊ a Jerusalɛm nɩbɛ, ‘Nyɛ, a yɩ nãa waara na a yɩ sɛ̃. Wʊ siir wʊ mãɛ na wʊ do bʊ̃ɔ. Bʊ̃pɔl bile lɛ wʊ do waara nɩ.’ ” (Jekariya

ref: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.

text: Wʊn wa kpɛ a Jerusalɛm tẽe pɔ, a nɩbɛ ba jaa ɩrɛ na yulõbʊmm, na ba sʊrɔ, “An lɛ a nyã?”

ref: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.

ref: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.

text: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, ‘A n yir na ɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ jie,’ (Isaiya

ref: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
text: Wʊ bar ba naa tɩ yi chen a Betanɩ tẽe pɔ tɩ gã a be.

ref: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “N mɩ na sʊʊr yɩn sʊʊrfʊ bʊ̃'yen. Yɩn wa tʊ̃ɔ wiil ma a par, n mɩ na wiil yɩ naa a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kʊ ma a kpɛ̃ɔ ka tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a anyãna.

ref: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
