ref: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
text: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩn tara sɔɔfʊ na yaa baal ɛ, a ba ɩ a lɛ na tʊ̃ kʊ a tɩɛ nyã tɛɛ lɛ yɩn tʊ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩn gba 'yɛr kʊ a tɔ̃ɔ nyãna, ‘Ir suu a man pɔ,’ tɩ wʊ̃n ir naa.

ref: So they told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus said, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” A Story about Two Sons
text: Be lɛ a ba 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, “Saa bɔ̃ɔ ɛ.” Ka a Yesu mɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “N mɩ kʊ̃ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kʊ ma a kpɛ̃ɔ ka n tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a anyãna ɛ.”

ref: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
text: “Yɩ lɛ chɛlɛ a sʊkpa ãsʊɔ. Daba lɛ kɔ wʊ puo na wʊ. Wʊ wɔɔn a puo wʊ jaa jilʊ̃, na wʊ tuu bɔɔ ka ban ma pɔr a wɔ̃mɔ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ na ba nɛŋmɛr ir a kʊ̃ɔ. Wʊ mɛn jie ãsʊɔ na do ka nɩrɛ na ma do jãnɛ a be kaara a puo. Wʊ den a puo kʊ nɩbɛ ɩka ba kaara tɩ na wʊ yɔ chen paal yuo pɔ.

ref: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
text: A Jɔɔn na dɔ̃ɔ suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, Nãaŋmɩn lɛ kʊ wʊ a sɔr bɩɩ nɩbɛ lɛ kʊ wʊ a sɔr? Yɩ 'yɛr n kaa?” Ka ba piel chɩrɛ taa, “Sɩn be 'yɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn lɛ tʊ̃ wʊ wʊn sʊʊr sɩ naa ka bo lɛ so ka saa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ?

ref: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
text: Tɩchɛ sɩn mɩ 'yɛr ɩka nɩrɛ lɛ tʊ̃ wʊ a ba ɩ ɛ, sɩ joro naa a nɩbɛ dãbãɛ bojũu nɩrɛ jaa sɔɔ naa ɩka a Jɔɔn ɩn Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.”

ref: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
text: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, ‘N kʊ̃ chen ɛ,’ tɩchɛ na wʊ ta sɔ̃ɔ lɩɛb a wʊ tɩɛr na wʊ chen.

ref: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
text: A sãa lɛ chen tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a ayi sʊɔ a lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ, ka wʊ 'yɛr, ‘N chen naa,’ tɩ na wʊ tɔr.

ref: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
text: “A bibiir banyãna ayi, buor sʊɔ lɛ tʊ̃ a wʊ sãa bɔfʊ?” Ka ba 'yɛr, “A dɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ lɛ.” Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ a lõpodierbe nɩ a pɔɔ baar na dɔ̃ɔ yɩn nie kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

ref: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
text: Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suoro wan wa wile yɩ a yelmãɛ sɔr ka yaa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a lõpodierbe nɩ a pɔɔ baar sɔɔn de wʊ, yɩn sɔɔ wa nyɛ gba yaa lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ.

ref: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
text: A pɔrfʊ sɔ̃ɔ na wa ta, wʊ tʊ̃n a wʊ gbãgbaar ɩka ba chen a nɩbɛ bala sɛ̃ tɩ pɔr a tɩɛ wɔ̃mɔ̃.

ref: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
text: “Tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ bala nyɔɔ a wʊ gbãgbaar ŋme a ãsʊɔ, na ba kʊ a ãsʊɔ tɩ na ba lɔb a ãsʊɔ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ kʊ.

ref: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
text: Be lɛ wʊ lɛ tʊ̃ a gbãgbaar bamɩne ka ba ge ba a lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju.

ref: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
text: A baaraa lɛ wʊ tʊ̃ a wʊ mãɛ bie a ba sɛ̃ na wʊ 'yɛr, ‘Ban 'yɔ̃ɔ naa a n bie.’

ref: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text: Be lɛ ba nyɔɔ wʊ kʊ lɔb bar a puo kõkoor.

ref: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
text: “Pʊ̃pãanyã a puo sʊɔ wa wa, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ wʊn ɩ a pokaarba banyãna?”

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
text: Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Wʊn wan wa kʊ a pokaarba banyã ba jaa tɩ de a puo kʊ nɩyor, a bala na ma sɔɔ ka ba põ a puo bonsɩ a ŋmafʊ sɔ̃ɔ.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
text: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yaa kar a jie nyã a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na 'yɛr ɩka a, ‘A kʊsɩr 'lɔ a mɩmɩɛrbɛ na tɔr bar lɛ ɩ a kʊsɩr ju. A sɩ Soro lɛ tʊ̃ a nɩtɔ̃ ka a sɔb sɩ a nɩbie.’ (Yiel Gãn

ref: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
text: “A lɛ jũu n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, Nãaŋmɩn na faan a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ yin a yɩ sɛ̃ kʊ a nɩyor bala na sɔɔ wʊ.

ref: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
text: Nɩrɛ nyã na lo a kʊsɩr nyã ju na pʊrpʊr a, tɩchɛ a wʊ mɩ na kʊ nɩrɛ nyã ju wʊn lo dɔɔl.”

ref: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
text: Ka ba bʊɔrɔ na sɔr ɩka ba nyɔɔ wʊ, tɩ na ba joro a nɩyɔɔ dãbãɛ bojũu ba nyɛn ka wʊ ɩ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.

ref: Many are invited, but only a few are chosen. Paying Taxes (Mark
text: “Nɩyɔɔ lɛ ba bʊɔl, tɩchɛ bãlãa lɛ ban chɛl ir.”

ref: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
text: Be lɛ ka a Faraseemɩne guor taa na ba wa bɛɛr Yesu kaa na ba na nyɔɔ wʊ a wʊ 'yɛrbie pɔ.

ref: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
text: “Wiwile, Mosesɩ sɛb ɩka yɛbɛ wa kpi tɩ bar pɔɔ na ba tara bie ɛ, a wʊ yɛbɛ wʊ kul a pɔɔkʊɔr 'lɔ na wʊ dɔɔ bibiir kʊ a wʊ yɛbɛ 'lɔ na kpi.

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
text: “A Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nyɛntãan fɩka nãa kɔ̃ɔ na maal a wʊ bidaba na kulo pɔɔ paalaa bʊ̃diir.

ref: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
text: “Wʊ lɛ tʊ̃n nɩyor ɩka ba tɩ 'yɛr a bala wʊn bʊɔl, ‘Nyɛ, n maal a pɔɔ paalaa bʊ̃diir baar, n kʊn a n nadar nɩ a napɔl ka yele jaa chɔɔr baar, yɩ wa a pɔɔ paalaa jie.’

ref: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
text: A nãa suur ir naa ka wʊ tʊ̃ a wʊ sojasɩ ɩka ba tɩ kʊ a nɩkʊrbɔ bala tɩ chʊm a ba tẽe.

ref: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
text: “Tɩ na wʊ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ, ‘Yele jaa chɔɔr baar, tɩchɛ a bala ɩn bʊɔl ba sɛɛ ɛ.

ref: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
text: Yɩ yi a tẽe pɔ tɩ bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa yɩn nyɛ ka wʊ wa a pɔɔ paalaa jie.’

ref: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
text: “Tɩchɛ a nãa na wa kpɛ ɩka wʊ kaa a nɩbɛ, wʊ nyɛn daba ãsʊɔ na ba su a pɔɔbɩɛl fuu ɛ,

ref: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
text: 'Yɛr kʊ sɩ a lɛ fʊn tɩɛr. A tara na sɔr ɩka sɩ yab a lõpoo kʊ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa bɩɩ sɩ ta yab ɛ?”

ref: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers and finally to all seven of them.
text: Ka a ayi sʊɔ mɩ de a pɔɔkʊɔr kul baa mɩ dɔɔ bie, tɩchɛ ka wʊ mɩ kpi. A lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ ata tɩ tãn a ayopõi sʊɔ.

ref: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
text: Be lɛ ba tʊ̃ a ba poturbo nɩ a nɩbɛ bamɩne na yi a Nãa Hɛrɔd sɛ̃, na ba 'yɛr, “Wiwile, sɩ bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka fʊ ɩn yelmãɛ sʊɔ, na fʊ wile Nãaŋmɩn sɔr nɩ yelmãɛ, bojũu faa kaara lɛ nɩbɛ na 'yɛrɛ bɩɩ oor ɛ.

ref: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
text: Ka ba 'yɛr, “A Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa lɛ.” Lɛ lɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ de a 'lɔ na ɩ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa sofʊ na yɩ kʊ wʊ, na yɩ mɩ de a 'lɔ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn sofʊ mɩ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn.”

ref: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark

text: A Mosesɩ nɛɛr a jaa nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ wiilfʊ kpãkpãana na a binfʊ nyãna.”

ref: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yaa nyɛ yɩn bɔr, bojũu yaa bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn gãma nɩ a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ ɛ.

ref: When God raises people to life, they won't marry. They will be like the angels in heaven.

ref: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,
text: Tɩchɛ a kũu pɔ ir fʊ yele, yaa kar a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɩka,

ref: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
text: n ɩ naa a Abraham Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a Isaakɩ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a Jekɔb Nãaŋmɩn ɛ? (A Yifʊ Gãn

ref: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark

ref: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.

ref: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
text: “Wiwile, A Mosesɩ nɛbinsɩ a jaa pɔ, buor lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ?”

text: Nyã lɛ ɩ a kpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a nɛdɔ̃ɔ.

ref: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”

ref: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
text: A Faraseemɩne na wa 'mataa, Yesu sʊʊr ba naa.

ref: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
text: ‘A Soro 'yɛr kʊ a n Soro, “Jãnɛ a n nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, ka n wa de a fʊ dɔ̃dẽme ka ba ɩ a fʊ gbɛɛ dɔɔlfʊ jie.” ’ (Yiel Gãn

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
text: “Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tɩɛr a Nɩ-iraa yele? An bie lɛ wʊ ɩ?” Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “David bie lɛ.”

ref: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
text: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, “Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ka a nãa David a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ, a wʊ bʊɔl wʊ ɩka, ‘N Soro’? Bojũu wʊ 'yɛr naa ɩka,

ref: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
text: Ala ɩ David bʊɔl wʊn wʊ ɩka, ‘N Soro,’ ŋmɩnɛ wʊn ɩrɛ wa ɩ a wʊ bie?”

ref: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
text: An yɩ a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ chiine, nɩrɛ jaa ba lɛ haa nɛɛ 'yɛr 'yɛr ɛ bɩɩ lɛ 'la sʊʊr sʊʊrfʊ jaa go ɛ.

ref: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na 'mɔɔrɔ a wʊ mãɛ ire nɩ, Nãaŋmɩn na suun wʊn pĩlem tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na siir wʊ mãɛ Nãaŋmɩn na 'mɔɔ wʊn ka wʊ do.

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.
text: A lɛ jũu n bar a fʊ tẽe kʊ fʊ ka wʊ ɩ dakpolo.

ref: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
text: A 'lɔ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ a yɩ kpɛ̃ɛ wʊ ɩ a yɩ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃.

ref: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
text: “Pa yɩn Jerusalɛm nɩbɛ, a yɩn na ma kʊrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na yɩ lɔɔrɔ a bala a Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ a yɩ sɛ̃ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ, nyɛ lɛ an ma ɩrɛ ma vɔlaa jã ka n ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka n kaal a fʊ bibiir 'mataa fɩka nʊ̃ɔ na ma ũun a wʊ bil, tɩchɛ ka faa bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ maal a lɛ ɛ.

ref: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
text: A lɛ jũu, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na pɔn a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie, pɔn wʊ naa nɩ bon jaa na dɔɔl a wʊ ju.

ref: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
text: Yesu pãa 'yɛr kʊ a nɩyɔɔ bala nɩ a wʊ poturbo,

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
text: “A nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne jãnɛ na a Mosesɩ dakɔɔ ju.

ref: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
text: A lɛ jũu, yɩ maala a lɛ ban wile tɩ na yɩ turo, tɩ yɩ ta maala a lɛ ban maala ɛ, bojũu ba maa turo a lɛ ban wile ɛ.

ref: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
text: Ba ma lũn tuor tɩɩr tuo nɩbɛ, tɩ maa sɔɔ de a ba nubir sɩɩr ɛ.

ref: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
text: “Yele jaa ban tʊ̃nɔ̃, ba ma bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka nɩbɛ ba nyɛ ba, na ba ma lũ a fɔɔr a ba jur pɔ, tɩ na ba ma su fuusɩ ban ma sɛ bonsɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ jaa vɛvɛɛ.

ref: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
text: Bala wa lɔ̃ɔ taa dire, a ji'yɔ̃ɔnsɩ jie lɛ ba ma nʊ̃ ɩka ba jãnɛ, nɩ a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir a jibɛrɛ jiir.

ref: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
text: Ala tɩ na ba ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka bama puoro ba a dar pɔ, na ba ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka nɩbɛ ba bʊɔlɔ ba, Wiwiilbe.

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: “Yɩ ta sɔɔ ka ba bʊɔlɔ yɩ, Wiwiilbe ɛ, nɩkpɛ̃ɛ bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ yɩ tara. Yɩ jaa ɩn yɛɛr.

ref: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
text: Yɩ ta bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa yɩ, sãa a wɛr pɔ ɛ, bojũu yɩ tara na Sãa bʊ̃'yen na be a saaju.

ref: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
text: Bɩɩ yɩ ta bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa wiwile ɛ, bojũu yɩ tara na Wiwile bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ na ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
text: “Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ ma yɔn jitɛbɛ nɩ a man pɔ na yɩ tɩ 'yɛr lɩɛb nɩrɛ tɩɛr, yɩn wa tʊ̃ɔ wʊ ka wʊ kaara a yɩ kaar, yɩ ma lɩɛb wʊ naa ka wʊn di dɔɔyɛ a bũu dagel pɔ a yɩ kaar.

ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
text: “Nɛbier ben a bala na ɩ jɔ̃n tɩ na ba tɔɔrɔ a jɔ̃n, na ba ma 'yɛr ɩka, ‘Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na pɔn a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir a ba ɩ tuo ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na pɔn a sɛlma 'lɔ na be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir a lũ wʊ naa.’

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Yɩ ɩn jɔ̃n na yɩ ɩ jɔlɔ. Buor lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ, a sɛlma bɩɩ a Nãaŋmɩn yir 'lɔ na maal a sɛlma ka wʊ ɩ a chɛchɛ?

ref: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
text: Yɩ mɩ 'yɛr naa, ‘Ala ɩ nɩrɛ pɔn na a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie, a ba tuo ɛ, tɩchɛ ala ɩ nɩrɛ pɔn na a Nãaŋmɩn kʊfʊ 'lɔ na dɔɔl a jie, a lũ wʊ naa.’

ref: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
text: Yɩn jɔ̃n banyã. Buor lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ, a kʊfʊ bɩɩ a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie na ma vɛ̃ ka wʊ ɩ chɛchɛ?

ref: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na pɔn a Nãaŋmɩn yir, pɔn wʊ naa nɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na jãnɛ a be.

ref: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na pɔn a saaju, pɔn naa a Nãaŋmɩn nãkɔɔ nɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na jãnɛ a be.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: “Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ ma ir a bonsɩ pɔ jiir pie pɔ bʊ̃'yen lɔ̃ɔn badʊʊ gba vaar kʊ Nãaŋmɩn, tɩ na yɩ tɔr a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛr ala na tɩrɛ bar na ɩ yelmãɛ sɔr tufʊ, nɩbaal kaafʊ nɩ yelmãɛ tarfʊ. A anyã lɛ a sɛɛ ɩka yɩ turo tɩ na yɩ taa ire amɩne bara ɛ.

ref: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
text: Yɩn jɔ̃tɔɔrbɔ ba nyã. Yɩ ma chuor a jãajubil bar tɩ ma vɔl a nyɔɔma.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
text: “Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ ma pɛɛn a ŋmanyuraa nɩ a ladiraa a yõo tɩchɛ ka a pʊɔ sɛɛn bɔgar nɩ nyuur.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: “Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ kaara fɩka yar ban pãɛ ka a vɩɛlɛ, tɩchɛ ka a pʊɔ pɔ tara nɩbɛ kɔbɔ nɩ dɛɛr jaa bʊrɔ.

ref: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
text: Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ a yɩn a yõo, yɩ ɩn nɩtor a yɩ taaba sɛ̃, tɩchɛ a yɩ pʊɔm, yɩ tara na nɛɛr ayi nɩ putuyele.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
text: “Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ mɛn a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yar, tɩ na chɔɔr a bala na tor yar,

ref: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
text: tɩ na yɩ 'yɛr ɩka, ‘Sɩn ta be be a sɩ sãakpãmɩne sɔ̃ɔ, sɩ kʊ̃ naa ta pʊɔ ba kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ɛ.’

ref: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
text: A nɩtɔ̃ jũu yɩ din a yɩ mãɛ dãasɩɛ ɩka yɩ ɩn a nɩbɛ bala na kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ bibiir.

ref: So keep on doing everything they did.
text: Yɩ baar baar a lɛ a yɩ sãakpãmɩne na dɔ̃ɔ ɩ.

ref: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
text: “Yɩn waa bibiir, ŋmɩnɛ yɩn ɩrɛ wa chãɛ a bũu dagel kũu.

ref: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ na nyɛn gefʊ a yele anyãna jũu.

ref: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka yɩ kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ ma go ɛ, wa tɩ tãn yɩn na 'yɛr ɩka, ‘Nãaŋmɩn wʊ sʊ̃ʊ a 'lɔ wʊn tʊ̃.’ ” (Yiel Gãn

ref: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
text: Yesu na wa yi a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir pɔ na wʊ chiine, a wʊ poturbo turo wile ɩka wʊ nyɛ lɛ ban mɛ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir ka wʊ kaara.

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Be lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Mɩ yɩ mɩ nyɛ naa anyãna a jaa? Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a kʊsɩvɩɛl anyãna ãsʊɔ jaa kʊ lɛ wa chɛnɛ dɔɔl a wʊ tɔ ju ɛ. Ban wa dãn a jaa lɔb.”

ref: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
text: Bojũu, nɩyɔɔ na de naa maa yuor waan wa 'yɛrɛ, ‘Maa lɛ a Nɩ-iraa,’ na ban bɛl nɩyɔɔ.

ref: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
text: Yɩn wa wõn tam, ka ba 'yɛrɛ a tam 'yɛr, yɩ ta mɩrɛ ɛ. A lɛ na ɩ naa, tɩchɛ a wɛr baarfʊ chɛnɛ na be puor.

ref: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
text: Yesu na wa do jãnɛ a Olive Tɔ̃ɔju, a poturbo wan a wʊ sɛ̃ a ba tɛɛ wa sʊʊr wʊ, “'Yɛr kʊ sɩ a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a anyãna nɩ na ɩ. Bo lɛ na ɩ a jãnɛ a fʊ waa sɔ̃ɔ nɩ a wɛr baarfʊ daar?”

ref: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
text: Paal dẽdẽme na yin tam, ka a nãmɩne mɩ yɩ tam ka kɔ̃ mɩ lo ka a tẽe jiir amɩne dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

ref: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: A bɩbir nyãna wa vɩɛ an ɩn tuo kʊ a pɔɔpʊʊr nɩ a bala na tara a bi'yɛnsɩ.

text: A lɛ daar nɩrɛ wa 'yɛr yɩ ɩka, ‘Nyɛ a Nɩ-iraa,’ bɩɩ ‘'Lɔ nɩ nyã,’ yɩ ta sɔɔ de ɛ.

ref: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
text: “Yɩn wa nyɛ naa a yele 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na 'laar na waara nɩ sɔ̃ɔfʊ ka wʊ be a jichɛchɛ 'lɔ nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Danɩɛl na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr. Vɛ̃ ka a nyã na kara bɔ̃ɔ a par. (Danɩɛl

text: 'Lɔ mɩ na be a puo pɔ wʊ ta kul a yir tɩ de a wʊ fuu ɛ.

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Ala ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn ba ŋmaa a ber ala bar ɛ, nɩrɛ kʊ̃ lɛ chɛnɛ go ɛ. Tɩchɛ a bala wʊn chɛl ir jũu wʊn ŋmaan a ber bar.

ref: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
text: A lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ, yɩn wa nyɛ a yelsɩ anyãna, yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a maa Nɩsaal Bie waa gbʊr naa jaa yɛ̃yɛ̃.

ref: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: A lɛ an dɔ̃ɔ kaara a Nowa sɔ̃ɔ, lɛ lɛ an mɩ na kaara a maa Nɩsaal Bie waa sɔ̃ɔ.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: “Nɩrɛ jaa ba bɔ̃ɔ a bɩbir nyã ɛ, bɩɩ a sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, a malakasɩ mɩ gba na be a saaju ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, bɩɩ a Bie ɛ, ka a Sãa tɛɛ ba lɛ bɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Bojũu sɛrɛ, tɩchɛ ka a kɔ̃paalaa wa nɩbɛ dire na, tɩ nyuro, ka dɔɔr nɩ pɔbɔ kulo taa, tɩ tãn a bɩbir 'lɔ a Nowa na kpɛ a daka bulomtum pɔ.

ref: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
text: Baa bɔ̃ɔn ɛ, tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a kɔ̃paalaa na wa di ba, lɛ mɩ lɛ an kaara a maa Nɩsaal Bie waa daar.

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Nɩbɛ ayi na ben puo pɔ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn wa de a ãsʊɔ chiin tɩ bar a ãsʊɔ.

ref: Two women will be together grinding grain, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Pɔbɔ ayi na 'mantaa nɩɛnɛ jɔ̃ ka a Nãaŋmɩn wa de a ãsʊɔ chiin tɩ bar a ãsʊɔ.

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: “A lɛ jũu yɩ kaara sʊ̃, bojũu yaa bɔ̃ɔ a bɩbir 'lɔ a yɩ Soro nɩ na wa ɛ.

ref: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
text: A lɛ jũu, yɩ mɩ guro a yɩ mãɛ, bojũu a maa Nɩsaal Bie na wan a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ yɩn kʊ̃ bɔ̃ɔn ɛ.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.

ref: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.

text: “A tãsɔɔ bãɩn lɛ ba ŋme chɛlma tãna 'yɛrɛ, ‘A pɔɔ paalaa sɩrɛ waara, yɩ yi wa tuor wʊ yooo.’

ref: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,

ref: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
text: “A mɩ lɛ nyɛntãan go fɩka daba kɔ̃ɔ na bʊɔrɔ yɔfʊ na wʊ bʊɔl a wʊ gbãgbaar 'mataa de a wʊ bonsɩ põ ba.

ref: As soon as the man had gone, the servant with the
text: A 'lɔ na de a sɛlbie anũu tɩ yar nyɛ sɛlbie anũu tʊ̃nɔ̃ dɔɔl.

text: “Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘Fʊ tʊ̃n vɩla, gbãgbamãɛ lɛ fʊ ɩ. Fʊ tara na yelmãɛ a bʊ̃bile pɔ, ɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊn ka fʊ kaara bʊ̃yɔɔ. Wa pʊɔ ma ka sɩ 'mataa dire a nʊ̃ɔ.’

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: “Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘Fʊ tʊ̃n vɩla, gbãgbamãɛ lɛ fʊ ɩ. Fʊ tara na yelmãɛ a bʊ̃bile pɔ, ɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊn ka fʊ kaara bʊ̃yɔɔ. Wa pʊɔ ma ka sɩ 'mataa dire a nʊ̃ɔ.’

ref: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”

ref: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
text: “Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘Fʊ̃ʊ gbãgbaputuo nɩ koro sʊɔ nyã. Fʊn ben bɔ̃ɔn ɩka n ma ŋman a be ɩn maa bʊr ɛ, naa wob a jie 'lɔ ɩn maa yaar bar ɛ,

text: “ ‘Yɩ faa a sɛlbir bʊ̃'yen a wʊ sɛ̃ na yɩ kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara a sɛlbie pie.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.

ref: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
text: A gbãgbaburbur nyã 'lɔ, yɩ de wʊ lɔb bar yõo be lɛ ban kõnõ tɩ ɔɔrɔ nyɩmɛ.’

ref: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
text: “A maa Nɩsaal Bie wa wa a n tɩɩr pɔ, nɩ a n malakasɩ ba jaa, ɩn wa jãnɛ naa, a n tɩɩr nãalʊ̃ʊ dakɔɔ ju.

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: A paalsɩ a jaa na pãa lɔ̃ɔ taa a n niem, ka n pãa oor a nɩbɛ nɩtãa lɛ pɛchinɛ na ma oor a piir nɩ a bʊʊr.

ref: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.

ref: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
text: “Ka n pãa 'yɛr kʊ a bala na be a n nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, ‘Yɩ wa, yɩn a n Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na sʊ̃ʊ. Yɩ wa kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ, de a yɩ nãbomo wʊn chɔɔr bin gun yɩ a wɛr pielfʊ sɔ̃ɔ.

ref: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
text: Bojũu kɔ̃ dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ma, ka yɩ kʊ ma ka n di, kɔ̃nyũur dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ma, ka yɩ kʊ ma kʊ̃ɔ ka n nyũ, n dɔ̃ɔ ɩ sãan ka yɩ ir ma sãan.

ref: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
text: N ba dɔ̃ɔ tara bʊ̃sursɩ ɛ, ka yɩ kʊ ma fuu ka n su, n dɔ̃ɔ bɩɛrɛ ka yɩ sãa ma. N dɔ̃ɔ be pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ka yɩ wa piir ma kaa.’

ref: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
text: “A nãa na 'yɛr kʊ ba, ‘Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a lɛ yɩn dɔ̃ɔ maal kʊ a nɩba'yɔ̃ɔnsɩ bala na ɩ a n sofʊ, maa lɛ yɩ maal kʊ.’

ref: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
text: “Tɩchɛ na wʊ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a bala mɩ na be a wʊ gʊbaa jie, ‘Pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ turo yɩ naa, yɩ tɔɔ chen be na yɩ tɩ kpɛ a bũu 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na gbɔɔ gun a tɩɩ nɩ a wʊ malakasɩ.

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
text: Bojũu kɔ̃ dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ma, ka yaa kʊ ma bon jaa ka n di ɛ. Kɔ̃nyũur dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ma, ka yaa kʊ ma kʊ̃ɔ ka n nyũ ɛ.

ref: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
text: N dɔ̃ɔ ɩ sãan, ka yaa kaa ma sãan kaa fʊ ɛ, n ba tara bon jaa na su ɛ, ka yaa kʊ ma fuu ka su ɛ, ka n bɩɛrɛ, bɩɩ be pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ka yaa wa piir ma kaa ɛ.’

ref: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
text: “Ka ba pãa mɩ lɩɛb sʊʊr wʊ, ‘Sɩ Soro, sɔ̃ɔ buor lɛ sɩ nyɛ fʊ, ka a kɔ̃ kpɛ fʊ, bɩɩ ka a kɔ̃nyũur kpɛ fʊ, bɩɩ ka fʊ ɩ sãan, bɩɩ ka faa tara bon jaa na su ɛ, bɩɩ ka fʊ bɩɛrɛ, bɩɩ ka fʊ be pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ka saa kaa fʊ ɛ?’

ref: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
text: “Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, ‘Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩn ba maal kʊ a nɩba'yɔ̃ɔnsɩ bala ãsʊɔ ɛ, maa lɛ yaa maal kʊ ɛ.’

ref: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
text: “A nɩbɛ banyãna na chen a dɔɔyɛ 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ jie, tɩchɛ ka a nɩmɩn mɩ chen a nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ jie.”

ref: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”
text: “Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a chɛn ber ayi ka a Gõl Bar Difʊ na ta, ka ba na de a maa Nɩsaal Bie tɩr ka ba kpa ma a daa ju.”

ref: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
text: Wʊ lɩɛb wa a poturbo sɛ̃ wa sʊʊr ba, “Yɩ chɛnɛ na gã guro na yɩ pɛnɛ wɛ? Nyɛ, a sɔ̃ɔ ta naa ka ban gar 'yɔ̃ɔ a maa Nɩsaal Bie de wʊ kʊ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ.

ref: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.
text: na ba bʊɔrɔ vuo ban na tu nyɔɔ a Yesu na ba kʊ.

ref: But they said, “We must not do it during Passover, because the people will riot.” At Bethany (Mark
text: Tɩchɛ ka ba 'yɛr, a ta ɩ a Gõl Bar Difʊ sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, ka jɛɛr taa ɩrɛ wa ir a chara pɔ ɛ.

ref: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”
text: Fʊn taa den a kãnyũu anyãna da libiyɔɔ de kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme.”

ref: But when his disciples saw this, they became angry and complained, “Why such a waste?

text: Ban wa yiel a yiel dãn Nãaŋmɩn, ba suun chen a Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ ju.

ref: and from then on he started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus. Jesus Eats the Passover Meal with His Disciples (Mark
text: An yɩ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ wʊ bʊɔrɔ na vuo wʊn na tu de a Yesu 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba nuur pɔ.

ref: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
text: Ban wa dire, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, a yɩ ãsʊɔ na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ ma.”

ref: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
text: A bɩbir dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ɩ naa a Paanʊ̃kõo Lɔ̃ɔfʊ, ka a Yesu poturbo wa sʊʊr wʊ, “Nyɩnɛ lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka sɩ chen tɩ chɔɔr a Gõl Bar nyã Difʊ bar ka sɩ tɩ di?”

ref: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
text: Ban wa dire, Yesu den a paanʊ̃ɔ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan na wʊ ŋma de kʊ a wʊ poturbo, tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr, “Yɩ de 'wɔb, a maa ãgãn lɛ.”

ref: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
text: A anyãna lɛ ɩ a n jãɩ na ɩ a nɛtɩraa ɩn tara nɩ yɩ na tara kpɛ̃ɔ. An kpaar na a faa nɩyɔɔ ka yelbier vɛ̃ kʊfʊ be be.

ref: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”

ref: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
text: “Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, sɩ lɛ tɩɛr a lɛ a jir ŋmara 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr wʊn dɔ̃ɔ chɛnɛ, ‘Ɩka ber ata daar ɩn lɛ lɩɛb ir ɩ vʊr.’

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: A lɛ jũu vɛ̃ ka ba gu a yaa tɩ tãn a ber ata daar, ka ala ba ɩ lɛ ɛ, a wʊ poturbo na chen tɩ ju a ãgãn a yaa pɔ, tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ ɩka, 'lɔ lɛ ir a kũu pɔ, ka a bɛlfʊ taa ɩ dɛɛr gar a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɛ.”

ref: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”

text: Yɩ chen fɔɔ jaa na yɩ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, ‘Wʊ lɩɛb ir a kũu pɔ, na wʊ de yɩ nie chiine a Galilee. Be lɛ yɩn tɩ nyɛ wʊ.’ N pãa 'yɛr kʊ yɩ.”

ref: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
text: A sojasɩ den a libie tɩ na ba ɩ a lɛ ban 'yɛr ɩka ba ɩ. A Juu nɩbɛ ma chɛnɛ na 'yɛr a yele nyãna a dɩna.

text: Ka a wʊ yele yaar a Galilee wɛr wʊ jaa.

ref: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
text: A ber ala pɔ Abiyata lɛ ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, kɔ̃ dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ba, ka wʊ kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn yir, ka a Abiyata de a paanʊ̃ɔ ala ban bin kʊ Nãaŋmɩn kʊ wʊ, ka wʊ 'wɔb. A paanʊ̃ɔ ala a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlorbo tɛɛ lɛ ma 'wɔb, a ba tara sɔr ɩka nɩyuo wʊ 'wɔb ɛ, tɩchɛ ka David 'wɔb tɩ de kʊ a bala na be a wʊ sɛ̃, ka ba mɩ 'wɔb.”

ref: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
text: Nɩyɔɔ wan wa duo na ba sɛɛ a die wa tɩ yin a yõo. Ka wa 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊrɔ ba.

ref: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
text: Be lɛ ka daba ãsʊɔ mɩ bɩɛrɛ, a wʊ nuur nɩ a wʊ gbɛɛ kpi naa, ka nɩbɛ anaar jɛ̃jɛl wʊ na ba waan a Yesu sɛ̃.

text: Be lɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir ben be a nɩbɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ, a ba ɩ a nɩbɛ ben be a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
text: A lõpodierbe nɩ a yelbetʊ̃nbɔ yɔɔ turo naa a Yesu, ka 'lɔ nɩ a bamɩne nɩ a wʊ poturbo 'mataa dire a Levi yir.

ref: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text: A Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe na ɩ a Faraseemɩne nyɛ a lɛ wʊn jãnɛ nɩ a lõpodierbe nɩ a yelbetʊ̃nbɔ 'mataa dire, ka ba sʊʊr a wʊ poturbo, “Bãa lɛ so a daba nyã na 'mataa dire nɩ a lõpodierbe nɩ a yelbetʊ̃nbɔ?”

ref: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Jɔɔn poturbo nɩ a Faraseemɩne nɩbɛ gal kɔ̃, ka nɩbɛ wa sʊʊr a Yesu, “Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a Jɔɔn poturbo nɩ a Farasee poturbo ma gal kɔ̃, tɩchɛ ka a fʊ̃ʊ poturbo maa gal kɔ̃ ɛ?”

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
text: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ an ɩ ka nɩbɛ be a pɔɔ paalaa sɩrɛ jie tɩ tʊ̃ɔ kɔ̃ gal? Wʊn chɛnɛ be a ba sɛ̃, ba kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

ref: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: A daar 'lɔ ban wa de a pɔɔ paalaa sɩrɛ, ban gal a kɔ̃.

ref: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: “Nɩrɛ maa ŋmaa fɔpaalaa sɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ fɔkoraa pɔ ɛ. 'Lɔ be sɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ a lɛ, a fɔpaalaa na tɔɔ naa a fɔkoraa chɩɛr.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
text: Nɩrɛ maa de dãbãɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ wɔkoraa pɔ ɛ. 'Lɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ, a wʊɔ na pur naa, ka a dãa nɩ a wʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ. Wʊ ma 'yɔ̃ɔ naa dãbãɩn wɔpaalaa pɔ.”

ref: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
text: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar, Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo kpɛ̃n tala puo sãlsɔɔ, ka a wʊ poturbo piel fɔrɔ a bʊ̃bie.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
text: Ka a Faraseemɩne 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, “Nyɛ lɛ a fʊ poturbo na ɩrɛ. Bo lɛ so ka ba tʊ̃nɔ̃ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir? A ba tara sɔr ɛ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Haven't you read what David did when he and his followers were hungry and in need?
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yaa bɔ̃ɔ a par a lɛ yɩn kar kaa a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ a nãa David nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ na ba dɔ̃ɔ tara bon na di ɛ!

ref: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
text: Ala lɛ so ka a Nɩsaal Bie ɩ a Soro lɔ̃ɔn a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir gba.”

ref: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
text: A nɩyɔɔ jũu, Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, “Yɩ kaa nyɛ gboro kʊ ma ka n sɛr bãlãa.” Wʊ 'yɛr naa a lɛ bojũu waa bʊɔrɔ ɩka a nɩbɛ ba mɩl pɔɔ wʊ ɛ.

ref: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
text: Ka a nɩbɛ bamɩne na be be bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba gar a Yesu, be lɛ ba tɔɔ gbʊr kaara wʊ. Ka wʊn sãa naa a daba nũu baal a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir bɩɩ.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a daba nũpɛl na kpi, “Ir ara a sɩ jaa nyɛ fʊ.”

ref: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
text: Wʊ kaa naa ba jaa gʊɔr nɩ suur, ka a wʊ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ a ba bachɛlfʊ jũu. Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a daba 'lɔ nũpɛl na kpi, “Tur a fʊ nũu.” Wʊ tur naa ka a nũpɛl wʊ jaa sa.

ref: Jesus led his disciples down to the shore of the lake. Large crowds followed him from Galilee, Judea,
text: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo yin chen a man pɔ ka a nɩyɔɔ na yi Galilee wɛr tu ba.

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Yesu do naa a tɔ̃ɔ ju tɩ bʊɔl a bala wʊn bʊɔrɔ ka ba do wa a wʊ sɛ̃.

ref: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One,
text: Andiru, Filip, Batolomi, Matiu, Tomasɩ, Jemesɩ na ɩ Alfayasɩ bidaba, Tadius, Simon na mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir nɩ a wʊ wɛr yele,

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Ka a Mosesɩ Nɛɛr wiwiilbe bamɩne na yi a Jerusalɛm mɩ 'yɛrɛ, “A Beeljebul, lɛ pɔb wʊ! A sɩdɛbɛ nãa lɛ wʊ ma de dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yire.”

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: Nɩyɔɔ jãnɛ naa jilʊ̃ a Yesu tɩ ba 'yɛr wʊ, “A fʊ ma nɩ a fʊ yɛɛr be naa a yõo bʊɔrɔ fʊ.”

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: Ban 'yɛr ɩka wʊ tara naa a sɩdɛɛr lɛ so ka wʊ 'yɛr a lɛ.

ref: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.

ref: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?

text: A anyã mɩ na lo a kɔjɔ̃ pɔ ɩ naa a bala na wõ a 'yɛr na ba sɔɔ de na ba dɔɔ jur, ka amɩne ɩ lɩjɛr nɩ pie, amɩne lɩjaata, amɩne mɩ kɔbaa.”

text: Ala wa bɩ baar, wʊ ma chiin nɩ sʊɔ tɩ ŋman bojũu a ŋmab sɔ̃ɔ ta naa.”

text: Tɩchɛ wʊ ma bul ɩ tɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wʊ tɛ wilbɛrɛ, ka a lubil ma wa mara a wʊ 'maar pɔ.”

text: Sʊkpar tɛɛ lɛ wʊ ma de 'yɛrɛ nɩ a 'yɛr kʊrɔ ba. Tɩchɛ ala wa ɩ 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ poturbo tɛɛ lɛ, wʊ ma wiil naa a jaa par kʊ ba.

text: Be lɛ ka wʊ ir tãn a sɛsɛbɛ nɩ a man, “Yɩ ŋmaa vʊʊr! Yɩ ara a lɛ nɛ!” Ka a sɛsɛbɛ ŋmaa vʊʊr ka a man ara jomm.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
text: Ka wʊ sʊʊr a wʊ poturbo, “Bo lɛ so ka yɩ joro a dãbãɛ? Yɩ chɛnɛ na ba sɔɔ ma bɩɩ?”

ref: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
text: Ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ ba ka ba lɩɛb sʊrɔ taa, “An lɛ a nyã ka a sɛsɛbɛ nɩ a man tu a wʊ nɛɛ?”

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: bojũu wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ mãɛ. “Maa tʊ̃ɔ sɩɩr a wʊ fuu gba, ɩn sa naa, na n pɛ̃n.”

ref: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “N yɛpɔɔ, fʊn sɔɔ ma jũu, fʊ sa naa. Kulo nɩ ãgãn 'maar, a fʊ dɔɔyɛ baar naa.”

ref: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
text: Wʊ bal di dɔɔyɛ yɔɔ a tɩtɩɩlbɛ jie na wʊ sɔ̃ɔ bon jaa wʊn tara tɩ ba sa ɛ. Ka a baal tʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ.

ref: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: Ka wʊ kpɛ tɩ sʊʊr ba, “Bãa lɛ yɩ kõnõ ŋmiere nɩ a chɛlsɩ a lɛ? A bie ba kpi ɛ. Wʊ gur naa.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo gɔ̃ɔ naa a man, a Gerasenɩ nɩbɛ wɛr pɔ.

ref: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
text: A gẽgelaa nyã kpɩɛrɛ naa a yar par ka nɩrɛ jaa ba be be na tʊ̃ɔ lũ wʊ nɩ bɔ̃jɔrsɩ gba ɛ.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.

ref: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!”
text: tɩ na wʊ ŋme chɛlma 'yɛrɛ, “Bo fʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ ɩ ma? Fʊ ɩ naa a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na gar a jaa Bie pɔn Nãaŋmɩn ɩka fʊ kʊ̃ ge ma ɛ.”

ref: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
text: Ka wʊ sʊɔr a Yesu yãayãabɩlɛ ɩka wʊ ta dii wʊ bar a jie 'lɔ ɛ.

text: Ka a sɩdɛbɛ sʊɔr a Yesu, “Ir sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a dor ala pɔ na fʊ sɔɔ ka sɩ tɩ kpɛ a ba pɔ.”

ref: Jesus let them go, and they went out of the man and into the pigs. The whole herd of about
text: Wʊ sɔɔ naa ka a sɩdɛbɛ yi tɩ kpɛ a dor ala. A ɩ naa dor tur ayi (

text: ) ka a jaa tĩgbil suu tɩ kpɛ a man pɔ gbʊ̃m kpi kpi.

ref: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
text: A bala na kaara a dor jon kul a tẽe pɔ nɩ a tẽbil ala na gbʊr tɩ 'yɛr a lɛ na ɩ. Ka a nɩbɛ yi chen ɩka ba tɩ kaa.

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Ba wa naa a Yesu sɛ̃ wa nyɛ a daba 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ tara a sɩdɛyɔɔ gele ka wʊ chɩ su bonsɩ jãnɛ, ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ ba.

ref: Everyone who had seen what had happened told about the man and the pigs.
text: A bala na nyɛ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ a lɛ na ɩ a daba na dɔ̃ɔ tara a sɩdɛbɛ nɩ a dor 'yɛr,

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
text: ka a nɩbɛ piel sʊɔrɔ a Yesu ka wʊ yi a ba wɛr pɔ.

text: Sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn wa bar a nɩyɔɔ bala tɩ kpɛ a yir, a wʊ poturbo sʊʊr wʊ naa a sʊkpa par.

ref: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
text: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, “Yɩ mɩ ɩ naa a nɩbɛ bala na ba bɔ̃ɔ a par bɩɩ? Yaa bɔ̃ɔ ɩka bon jaa ba be be ka nɩrɛ na de di na wʊ dɛɛ ɛ,

ref: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
text: bojũu wʊ maa kpɛ a wʊ tɩɛr pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ pʊɔm lɛ wʊ ma suu na wʊ tɩ yi.” Yesu 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ na wʊ wiil ɩka a bʊ̃diir a jaa sɛɛn difʊ.

ref: unfaithfulness in marriage, greed, meanness, deceit, indecency, envy, insults, pride, and foolishness.
text: nɩ a nyuur, nɩ a putuo, nɩ jire, nɩ kakaba, nɩ bɔbier, nɩ yosɔ̃ɔ, nɩ pɔlyele nɩ a jɔlyele.

ref: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
text: A yeldɛbɛ anyãna a jaa lɛ ma yi a nɩrɛ pʊtɩɛr pɔ, vɛ̃ ka wʊ dɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
text: Be lɛ ka a Yesu yi a be na wʊ chen a Tire wɛr pɔ. Wʊ tɩ kpɛ naa a yir ãsʊɔ pɔ, tɩ waa bʊɔrɔ ɩka nɩrɛ jaa wʊ bɔ̃ɔ ka wʊ ben be ɛ. Tɩchɛ wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ sɔɔl ɛ.

ref: A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet.
text: Ala a pɔɔ na tara wʊ bipɔɔ ka a sɩdɛɛr tara, ajienaa wʊn wʊ wõ a Yesu 'yɛr, wʊ wan wa lo gbur dumo a wʊ niem.

ref: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
text: A pɔɔ ba dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ ɛ, na wʊ dɔ̃ɔ yi be a Siriya Fonisiya paal pɔ. Wʊ sʊɔr naa a Yesu ɩka, wʊ dii a sɩdɛɛr bar a wʊ bipɔɔ pɔ.

ref: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “Bar ka a bibiir di a lɛ ban bʊɔrɔ, taa de a bibiir bʊ̃diir kʊ baar ɛ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “That's true! You may go now. The demon has left your daughter.”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “A nɩtɔ̃ fʊn 'yɛr jũu, kulo. A sɩdɛɛr bar naa a fʊ bipɔɔ.”

ref: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
text: Wʊ kul tɩ nyɛ a bie na gã a gado ju, tɩ a sɩdɛɛr yi.

ref: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
text: Yesu lɛ yi naa a Tire wɛr pɔ, na wʊ tu a Sidon suu a Galilee man pɔ, na wʊ tɩ chen a Dekapolisɩ paal pɔ.

ref: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus just to touch him.
text: Be lɛ ka nɩbɛ bamɩne waan daba na ɩ wõo, na waa tʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ 'yɛrɛ sʊ̃ ɛ. Ba sʊɔr a Yesu ka wʊ sɩɩr a daba nɩ a wʊ nũu na wʊ sãa wʊ.

ref: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
text: Ka Yesu tara a daba yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ, na wʊ de a wʊ nubie su a daba tobɔɔr pɔ, be lɛ wʊ chɩr nataar tɩ sɩɩr a daba jãlbir.

ref: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
text: Na wʊ dʊl kaa a saaju tɩ vʊʊr na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a daba, “Efata!”, a par lɛ a Yesu nɩbɛ kɔkɔr pɔ, yuo.

ref: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
text: Ajienaa ka a daba tobo yuo ka a jãlbir lɩɛb ka wʊ piel 'yɛrɛ jaa chɛchɛ.

ref: Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it.
text: Yesu kpaal ban jũu ɩka ba taa ɩrɛ wa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ nɩrɛ jaa ɛ. Tɩchɛ a lɛ wʊn kpãa ba lɛ lɛ ba mɩ 'yɛrɛ yʊɔrɔ nɩ a jie wʊ jaa.

ref: They were completely amazed and said, “Everything he does is good! He even heals people who cannot hear or talk.”
text: A nɩbɛ nɛɛ 'maan yɔɔ jaa ka ba 'yɛrɛ, “Wʊ ɩn yele jaa sʊ̃. Wʊ gba ma vɛ̃ naa ka wõo wõnõ, tɩ jãlbir lɩɛb 'yɛrɛ chɛchɛ.”

ref: Jesus warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod.”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a Faraseemɩne paanʊ̃ɔ dãbɩl nɩ a Hɛrɔd paanʊ̃ɔ dãbɩl mɩ.”

ref: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
text: Wʊ 'yɛr naa anyã chãa pɔ, ka a Pita tara wʊ yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ tɩ piel mii 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ.

ref: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
text: “A nɩbɛ banyã ɩrɛ naa nɩbaal, bojũu ber ata nɩ nyã ban be a n sɛ̃, baa lɛ tara bon na di ɛ.

ref: Some of them live a long way from here. If I send them away hungry, they might faint on their way home.”
text: Maa mɩ bar ba ka ba kulo nɩ a kɔ̃, ban tɩ lon sɔr pɔ, bojũu bamɩne yin tɛɛr.”

ref: After Jesus told the crowd to sit down, he took the seven loaves and gave thanks. He then broke the loaves and handed them to his disciples, who passed them out to the crowd.
text: Wʊ 'yɛr naa a nɩbɛ, ka ba jãnɛ tẽe, tɩ na wʊ de a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ ayopõi ala puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, tɩ ŋma ŋma de kʊ a wʊ poturbo ka ba de põ a nɩbɛ.

ref: They also had a few little fish, and after Jesus had blessed these, he told the disciples to pass them around.
text: Ba tara naa a jãbil amɩne, ka wʊ de a ala mɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, tɩ 'yɛr ɩka a poturbo ba de põ a nɩbɛ.

ref: he got into the boat with the disciples and crossed to the territory near Dalmanutha. A Sign from Heaven (Matthew
text: Ajienaa, 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ poturbo 'mantaa kpɛ a gboro pɔ, na ba chen a Dalmanuta paal pɔ.

ref: The Pharisees came out and started an argument with Jesus. They wanted to test him by asking for a sign from heaven.
text: Be lɛ a Faraseemɩne wa sʊrɔ wʊ yelsʊʊrsɩ, bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba bɛɛr wʊ kaa, na ba 'yɛr ɩka wʊ wiil ba jãnɛ kɔ̃ɔ ka ba bɔ̃ɔ ɩka wʊ yin a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

ref: The disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
text: A wʊ poturbo ĩin naa ba tara paanʊ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩ tara bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ a gboro pɔ.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking and asked, “Why are you talking about not having any bread? Don't you understand? Are your minds still closed?
text: Yesu na bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban 'yɛrɛ, wʊ sʊʊr ba naa, “Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ yɩ 'yɛrɛ ɩka yaa tara paanʊ̃ɔ ɛ? Yaa bɔ̃ɔ a par sɛrɛ ɛ? A yɩ nyãa kpɛ̃mɛ na mɩ?

ref: Jesus took him by the hand and led him out of the village, where he spit into the man's eyes. He placed his hands on the blind man and asked him if he could see anything.
text: Yesu nyɔɔ naa a jɔ̃ɔ nũu tɩ tɔɔ wʊ yin a tẽe chɛchɛ. Wʊ tɩ chɩr nataar 'yɔ̃ɔ a daba nɩbie pɔ tɩ dɔɔl wʊ a nũu tɩ sʊʊr wʊ, “Fʊ nyɛn bon bɩɩ?”

ref: The man looked up and said, “I see people, but they look like trees walking around.”
text: Ka a daba dʊɔ kaa tɩ 'yɛr, “N nyɛn nɩbɛ na kaara tɩɩr kaar na ba vire yʊɔrɔ.”

ref: Once again Jesus placed his hands on the man's eyes, and this time the man stared. His eyes were healed, and he saw everything clearly.
text: Ka a Yesu lɛ de a wʊ nuur pɔɔ a daba nɩbie, ka a wʊ nɩbie yuo, ka wʊ nyɛrɛ pãa nyɛ bomo a jaa chɛchɛ.

ref: Jesus said to him, “You may return home now, but don't go into the village.” Who Is Jesus? (Matthew
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Kulo, ta lɛ kpɛ a tẽe pɔ ɛ.”
